name: Alchemist
description: An Alchemist is an expert at combining reagents to produce magical effects. Alchemists use their creations to give life and to leech it away.

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.toolProficiency: You gain proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} and the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}. If you already have one of these proficiencies, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice (or with two other types if you have both).
details.potionCrafting: When you brew a potion using the crafting rules in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG}, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

features.name: Alchemist Spells
features.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Alchemist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Experimental Elixir
features.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} while holding {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, you can use that tool to magically produce two elixirs. For each elixir, roll on the Experimental Elixir table for the elixir's effect, which is triggered when someone drinks the elixir. The elixir appears in a vial, and the vial vanishes when the elixir is drunk or poured out. If any elixir remains when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the elixir and its vial vanish.

details.drinkingAnElixir: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, a creature can drink the elixir or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of itself.
details.creatingAdditionalElixirs: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while holding {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, you can expend one spell slot to create another elixir. When you do so, you choose its effect from the Experimental Elixir table rather than rolling.
details.additionalElixirsPerRest: When you reach certain Artificer levels, you can make an additional elixir at the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}: a total of three at level 5, four at level 9, and five at level 15.

experimentalElixirTable.name: Healing
experimentalElixirTable.effect: The drinker regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} plus your Intelligence modifier. The number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach Artificer levels 9 ({@dice 3d6}) and 15 ({@dice 4d6}).

experimentalElixirTable.name: Swiftness
experimentalElixirTable.effect: The drinker's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet for 1 hour. This bonus increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (15 feet) and 15 (20 feet).

experimentalElixirTable.name: Resilience
experimentalElixirTable.effect: The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for 10 minutes. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (1 hour) and 15 (8 hours).

experimentalElixirTable.name: Boldness
experimentalElixirTable.effect: The drinker can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to every attack roll and saving throw they make for the next minute. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (10 minutes) and 15 (1 hour).

experimentalElixirTable.name: Flight
experimentalElixirTable.effect: The drinker gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 10 feet for 10 minutes. The {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (20 feet) and 15 (30 feet).

experimentalElixirTable.name: Transformation
experimentalElixirTable.effect: You determine the elixir's effect by choosing one of the other rows in this table.

features.name: Alchemical Savant
features.description: Whenever you cast a spell using your {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} as the {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}, you gain a bonus to one roll of the spell. That roll must restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or be a damage roll that deals Acid, Fire, or Poison damage. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum bonus of +1).

features.name: Restorative Reagents
features.description: You can cast {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} without expending a spell slot and without preparing the spell, provided you use {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} as the {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Chemical Mastery
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.alchemicalEruption: When you cast an Artificer spell that deals Acid, Fire, or Poison damage to a target, you can also deal {@damage 2d8} Force damage to that target. You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns.
details.chemicalResistance: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid damage and Poison damage. You also gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
details.conjuredCauldron: You can cast {@spell Tasha's Bubbling Cauldron|XPHB} without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without Material components, provided you use {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} as the {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Armorer
description: An Armorer modifies armor to function almost like a second skin. The armor is enhanced to hone the Armorer's magic, unleash potent attacks, and generate a formidable defense.
shortDescription: Craft Magic Armor to Enhance Your Abilities

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.armorTraining: You gain training with Heavy armor.
details.toolProficiency: You gain proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have this tool proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.
details.armorCrafting: When you craft nonmagical or magic armor, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

features.name: Armorer Spells
features.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Armorer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Arcane Armor
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while you have {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} in hand, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor. The armor continues to be Arcane Armor until you don another suit of armor or you die. You gain the following benefits while wearing your Arcane Armor.

details.noStrengthRequirement: If the armor normally has a Strength requirement, the Arcane Armor lacks this requirement for you.
details.quickDonAndDoff: You can don or doff the armor as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. The armor can't be removed against your will.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use the Arcane Armor as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Artificer spells.

features.name: Armor Model
features.description: You can customize your Arcane Armor. When you do so, choose one of the following armor models: Dreadnaught, Guardian, or Infiltrator. The model you choose gives you special benefits while you wear it. Each model includes a special weapon. When you attack with that weapon, you can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, to the attack and damage rolls. You can change the armor's model whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} if you have {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} in hand.

dreadnaught.name: Dreadnaught
dreadnaught.description: You design your armor to become a towering juggernaut in battle. It has the following features:

features.forceDemolisher: An arcane wrecking ball or sledgehammer projects from your armor. The demolisher counts as a Simple Melee weapon with the {@variantrule Reach|XPHB} property, and it deals {@damage 1d10} Force damage on a hit. If you hit a creature that is at least one size smaller than you with the demolisher, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself or pull the creature up to 10 feet toward yourself.
features.giantStature: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can transform and enlarge your armor for 1 minute. For the duration, your reach increases by 5 feet, and if you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If there isn't enough room for you to increase your size, your size doesn't change. You can use this {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

guardian.name: Guardian
guardian.description: You design your armor to be in the front line of conflict. It has the following features:

features.thunderPulse: You can discharge concussive blasts with strikes from your armor. The pulse counts as a Simple Melee weapon and deals {@damage 1d8} Thunder damage on a hit. A creature hit by the pulse has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against targets other than you until the start of your next turn.
features.defensiveField: While Bloodied, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Artificer level. You lose these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} if you doff the armor.

infiltrator.name: Infiltrator
infiltrator.description: You customize your armor for subtle undertakings. It has the following features:

features.lightningLauncher: A gemlike node appears on your armor, from which you can shoot bolts of lightning. The launcher counts as a Simple Ranged weapon with a normal range of 90 feet and a long range of 300 feet, and it deals {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with the launcher, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage to that target.
features.dampeningField: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. If the armor imposes {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on such checks, the {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} and {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} cancel each other, as normal.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

features.name: Improved Armorer
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.armorReplication: You learn an additional plan for your Replicate Magic Item feature, and it must be in the Armor category. If you replace that plan, you must replace it with another Armor plan.
details.improvedArsenal: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with the special weapon of your Arcane Armor model.

features.name: Perfected Armor
features.description: Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model, as detailed below.

dreadnaught.name: Dreadnaught
dreadnaught.description: The damage die of your Force Demolisher increases to {@damage 2d6} Force damage. In addition, when you use your Giant Stature, your reach increases by 10 feet, your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice), and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws for the duration.

guardian.name: Guardian
guardian.description: The damage die of your Thunder Pulse increases to {@damage 1d10} Thunder damage. In addition, when a Huge or smaller creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to magically force that creature to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you pull the creature up to 25 feet directly toward you to an unoccupied space. If you pull the target to a space within 5 feet of yourself, you can make a melee weapon attack against it as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. You can take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

infiltrator.name: Infiltrator
infiltrator.description: The damage die of your Lightning Launcher increases to {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage. Any creature that takes Lightning damage from your Lightning Launcher glimmers with magical light until the start of your next turn. The glimmering creature sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius, and it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you, as the light jolts it if it attacks you. Additionally, as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} until the end of the current turn. You can take this {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Artillerist
description: An Artillerist specializes in using magic to hurl energy, projectiles, and explosions on a battlefield.

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.rangedWeaponry: You gain proficiency with Martial Ranged weapons.
details.toolProficiency: You gain proficiency with {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.
details.wandCrafting: When you craft a magic {@item Wand|XPHB}, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

features.name: Artillerist Spells
features.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Artillerist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Eldritch Cannon
features.description: Using {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB}, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to create a Small or Tiny {@creature Eldritch Cannon|EFA} in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of yourself. You determine its appearance, including whether you carry it or not (and your choice of legs or wheels, for the latter). It disappears if it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or after 1 hour. You can dismiss it early as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. Once you create a cannon, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or expend a spell slot to create one. You can have only one cannon at a time and can't create one while you already have one.

features.name: Arcane Firearm
features.description: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB} to carve special sigils into a Rod, Staff, {@item Wand|XPHB}, or Martial Ranged weapon and thereby turn it into your Arcane Firearm. The sigils disappear from the object if you later carve them on a different item. The sigils otherwise last indefinitely. You can use your Arcane Firearm as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Artificer spells. When you cast an Artificer spell through the firearm, roll {@dice 1d8}, and you gain a bonus to one of the spell's damage rolls equal to the number rolled.

features.name: Explosive Cannon
features.description: Every {@creature Eldritch Cannon|EFA} you create is now more destructive. You gain the following benefits.

details.detonate: When your cannon takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to command the cannon to detonate if you are within 60 feet of it. Doing so destroys the cannon and forces each creature within 20 feet of it to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 3d10} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
details.firepower: The cannon's damage rolls and the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by Protector increase by {@dice 1d8}.

features.name: Fortified Position
features.description: You're a master at forming well-defended emplacements using your {@creature Eldritch Cannon|EFA}. You gain the following benefits.

details.doubleFirepower: You can now have two cannons at the same time, and you can create two with the same {@action Magic|XPHB} action. (If you expend a spell slot to create the first cannon, you must expend another spell slot to create the second.) You can activate both of them with the same {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, ordering them to use the same activation option or different ones. You can't create a third cannon while you have two.
details.shimmeringFieldProjection: You and your allies have {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} within 10 feet of your {@creature Eldritch Cannon|EFA}.

name: Battle Smith
description: A Battle Smith is a combination of protector and medic, an expert at defending others and repairing both materiel and personnel. To aid in their work, Battle Smiths are accompanied by a {@creature Steel Defender|EFA}, a protective companion of their own creation.

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.toolProficiency: You gain proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.
details.weaponCrafting: When you craft a nonmagical or magic weapon, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

features.name: Battle Smith Spells
features.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Battle Smith Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Battle Ready
features.description: Your combat training and your experiments with magic have paid off in two ways.

details.arcaneEmpowerment: When you attack with a magic weapon, you can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, for the attack and damage rolls.
details.weaponKnowledge: You gain proficiency with {@variantrule Martial weapons|XPHB}. You can use a weapon with which you have proficiency as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Artificer spells.

features.name: Steel Defender
features.description: Your tinkering has borne you a companion, a {@creature Steel Defender|EFA}. You determine the defender's appearance and whether it has two legs or four; your choices don't affect the defender's game statistics. It vanishes if you die.

details.friendly: The {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} is {@variantrule Friendly|XPHB} to you and your allies and obeys you.
details.defenderInCombat: In combat, the defender acts during your turn. It can move and take its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take an action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the defender acts on its own and isn't limited to the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.
details.restoringOrReplacing: If the defender has died within the last hour, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch it and expend a spell slot. The defender returns to life after 1 minute with all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create a new defender if you have {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} in hand. If you already have a defender from this feature, the first one vanishes.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. You can forgo one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to command your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} to take the Force-Empowered Rend action.

features.name: Arcane Jolt
features.description: When either you hit a target with an attack roll using a magic weapon or your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} hits a target, you can channel magical energy through the strike to create one of the following effects:

details.destructiveEnergy: The target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Force damage.
details.restorativeEnergy: Choose one creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target. Healing energy flows into the chosen recipient, restoring {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to it.
details.uses: You can use this energy a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), but you can do so no more than once per turn. You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Improved Defender
features.description: Your Arcane Jolt and {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} have become more powerful, granting these benefits.

details.improvedJolt: The extra damage and healing of your Arcane Jolt both increase to {@dice 4d6}.
details.improvedDeflection: Whenever your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} uses its Deflect Attack, the attacker takes Force damage equal to {@dice 1d4} plus your Intelligence modifier.

name: Cartographer
description: Cartographers are the premier navigators and reconnaissance agents. Using their creations, Cartographers can highlight threats, safeguard allies, and carve portals to distant locations.

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.toolProficiency: You gain proficiency with {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB} and {@item Cartographer's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have one of these proficiencies, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice (or with two other types if you have both).
details.scrollCrafting: When you scribe a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} using the crafting rules in the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

features.name: Cartographer Spells
features.description: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Cartographer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Adventurer's Atlas
features.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} while holding {@item Cartographer's Tools|XPHB}, you can use that tool to create a set of magical maps by touching at least two creatures (one of whom can be yourself), up to a maximum number of creatures equal to 1 plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum of two creatures). Each target receives a magical map, which constantly updates to show the relative position of all the map holders but is illegible to all others. The maps last until you die or until you use this feature again, at which point any existing maps created by this feature immediately vanish.

details.awareness: The target adds {@dice 1d4} to its {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.
details.positioning: The target knows the location of all other map holders that are on the same plane of existence as itself. When casting a spell or creating another effect that requires being able to see the effect's target, a map holder can target another map holder regardless of sight or cover, so long as the other map holder is still within the effect's range.

features.name: Mapping Magic
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.illuminatedCartography: You can cast {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} without expending a spell slot, outlining the affected creatures as if in ink. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.portalJump: On your turn, you can spend an amount of movement equal to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} (round down) to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of yourself or within 5 feet of a creature that is within 30 feet of you and holding one of your Adventurer's Atlas maps. You can't use this benefit if your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0.

features.name: Guided Precision
features.description: Once per turn, whenever you cast a spell from your Cartographer Spells list or hit a creature affected by your {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} with an attack roll, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of the spell or attack. In addition, taking damage can't cause you to lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB}.

features.name: Ingenious Movement
features.description: When you use your Flash of Genius, you or a willing creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of yourself can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see as part of that same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

features.name: Strategic Atlas
features.description: Your Adventurer's Atlas improves, gaining the following benefits.

details.safeHaven: When a map holder would be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, that creature can destroy its map. The creature's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead change to a number equal to twice your Artificer level, and the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you or another map holder of its choice.
details.unerringPath: If you are one of the map holders for your Adventurer's Atlas, you can cast {@spell Find the Path|XPHB} without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without needing spell components. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Path of the Ancestral Guardian
description: Some barbarians hail from cultures that revere their ancestors. These tribes teach that the warriors of the past linger in the world as mighty spirits, who can guide and protect the living. When a barbarian who follows this path rages, the barbarian contacts the spirit world and calls on these guardian spirits for aid.
shortDescription: Summon Ancestral Spirits to Protect Allies
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Ancestral Protectors
features.description: Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, spectral warriors appear when you enter your rage. While you're raging, the first creature you hit with an attack on your turn becomes the target of the warriors, which hinder its attacks. Until the start of your next turn, that target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any attack roll that isn't against you, and when the target hits a creature other than you with an attack, that creature has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage dealt by the attack. The effect on the target ends early if your rage ends.

features.name: Spirit Shield
features.description: Beginning at 6th level, the guardian spirits that aid you can provide supernatural protection to those you defend. If you are raging and another creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by {@dice 2d6}. When you reach certain levels in this class, you can reduce the damage by more: by {@dice 3d6} at 10th level and by {@dice 4d6} at 14th level.

features.name: Consult the Spirits
features.description: At 10th level, you gain the ability to consult with your ancestral spirits. When you do so, you cast the {@spell augury|XPHB} or {@spell clairvoyance|XPHB} spell, without using a spell slot or material components. Rather than creating a spherical sensor, this use of {@spell clairvoyance|XPHB} invisibly summons one of your ancestral spirits to the chosen location. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells. After you cast either spell in this way, you can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Vengeful Ancestors
features.description: At 14th level, your ancestral spirits grow powerful enough to retaliate. When you use your Spirit Shield to reduce the damage of an attack, the attacker takes an amount of force damage equal to the damage that your Spirit Shield prevents.

name: Path of the Battlerager
description: Known as Kuldjargh (literally "axe idiot") in Dwarvish, battleragers are dwarf followers of the gods of war and take the Path of the Battlerager. They specialize in wearing bulky, {@item spiked armor|XPHB} and throwing themselves into combat, striking with their body itself and giving themselves over to the fury of battle.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.
restriction: Only dwarves can follow the Path of the Battlerager. The battlerager fills a particular niche in dwarven society and culture. Your DM can lift this restriction to better suit the campaign.

features.name: Battlerager Armor
features.description: When you choose this path at 3rd level, you gain the ability to use {@item spiked armor|XPHB} as a weapon. While you are wearing {@item spiked armor|XPHB} and are raging, you can use a bonus action to make one melee weapon attack with your armor spikes at a target within 5 feet of you. If the attack hits, the spikes deal {@damage 1d4} piercing damage. You use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. Additionally, when you use the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to grapple a creature, the target takes {@damage 1d4} piercing damage if your grapple check succeeds.

features.name: Reckless Abandon
features.description: Beginning at 6th level, when you use Reckless Attack while raging, you also gain temporary hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). They vanish if any of them are left when your rage ends.

features.name: Battlerager Charge
features.description: Beginning at 10th level, you can take the Dash action as a bonus action while you are raging.

features.name: Spiked Retribution
features.description: Starting at 14th level, when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the attacker takes 3 piercing damage if you are raging, aren't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}, and are wearing {@item spiked armor|XPHB}.

name: Path of the Beast
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Beast draw their rage from a bestial spark burning within their souls. That beast bursts forth in the throes of rage, physically transforming the barbarian.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Form of the Beast
features.description: When you enter your rage, you can transform, revealing the bestial power within you. Until the rage ends, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you add your Strength modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it, as normal. You choose the weapon's form each time you rage.

originOfTheBeast.origin: One of your parents is a lycanthrope, and you've inherited some of their curse.
You are descended from an archdruid and inherited the ability to partially change shape.
A fey spirit gifted you with the ability to adopt different bestial aspects.
An ancient animal spirit dwells within you, allowing you to walk this path.

details.bite: Your mouth transforms into a bestial muzzle or great mandibles (your choice). It deals {@damage 1d8} piercing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you damage a creature with this bite, you regain a number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus, provided you have less than half your hit points when you hit.
details.claws: Each of your hands transforms into a claw, which you can use as a weapon if it's empty. It deals {@damage 1d6} slashing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you attack with a claw using the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make one additional claw attack as part of the same action.
details.tail: You grow a lashing, spiny tail, which deals {@damage 1d8} piercing damage on a hit and has the reach property. If a creature you can see within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack roll, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to swipe your tail and roll a {@dice d8}, applying a bonus to your AC equal to the number rolled, potentially causing the attack to miss you.

features.name: Bestial Soul
features.description: The feral power within you increases, causing the natural weapons of your Form of the Beast to count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. You can also alter your form to help you adapt to your surroundings. When you finish a short or long rest, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you finish your next short or long rest.

details.swimming: You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed, and you can breathe underwater.
details.climbing: You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
details.jumping: When you jump, you can make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check and extend your jump by a number of feet equal to the check's total. You can make this special check only once per turn.

features.name: Infectious Fury
features.description: When you hit a creature with your natural weapons while you are raging, the beast within you can curse your target with rabid fury. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus) or suffer one of the following effects (your choice).

details.forcedAttack: The target must use its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature of your choice that you can see.

features.name: Call the Hunt
features.description: The beast within you grows so powerful that you can spread its ferocity to others and gain resilience from them joining your hunt. When you enter your rage, you can choose a number of other willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of one creature). You gain 5 temporary hit points for each creature that accepts this feature. Until the rage ends, the chosen creatures can each use the following benefit once on each of their turns: when the creature hits a target with an attack roll and deals damage to it, the creature can roll a {@dice d6} and gain a bonus to the damage equal to the number rolled. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Path of the Berserker
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Berserker direct their Rage primarily toward violence. Their path is one of untrammeled fury, and they thrill in the chaos of battle as they allow their Rage to seize and empower them.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. The damage has the same type as the weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} used for the attack.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions while your Rage is active. If you're {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} when you enter your Rage, the condition ends on you.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, a creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature's turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a use of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

name: Path of the Fractured
description: Barbarians are defined by their rage, channelling it to unleash brief but potent destruction. Few are brave or unwise enough to study esoteric psychological techniques that split their rage from the rest of their psyche, dividing their identity into two parts: ego and id. When their ego is in control, the Fractured—as these Barbarians are known—possess self-control and cunning beyond most other Barbarians. When they allow their id to take control, their countenance turns monstrous and their bodies swell with the power of rage made physically manifest.
shortDescription: Two Personalities Are Better than One

features.name: Face of Rage
features.description: When you activate your Rage, your countenance distorts and your body swells. Creatures that haven't witnessed your transformation, now or previously, don't recognize you. In addition, while your Rage is active, you gain the following benefits:

details.unarmedStrikeDamage: You can roll {@dice 1d8} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, and whenever you deal damage with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.
details.push: When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can push it 10 feet or force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
details.sizeAndReach: You count as one size larger when determining the success or failure of a Grapple, and when you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, your reach is 5 feet greater than normal.

features.name: Mask of Civility
features.description: You are proficient in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, Persuasion, or Religion. In addition, you gain proficiency with one type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice or you know one language of your choice.

features.name: Brains and Brawn
features.description: While your Rage is not active, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage. While your Rage is active, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force and Psychic.

features.name: Cunning and Brutal
features.description: While your Rage is not active, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} or {@action Help|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. While your Rage is active, your attack rolls with {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

features.name: Better Half
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} instead, and you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum. In addition, if your Rage is active, your Rage ends. If your Rage was not active, immediately activate your Rage (even if you have no remaining uses of your Rage). If any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain after 1 minute, they vanish. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Path of the Giant
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Giant draw strength from the same primal forces as giants. As they rage, these barbarians surge with elemental power and grow in size, taking on forms that evoke the glory of giants.
shortDescription: Channel the Primal Might of Giants
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Giant Power
features.description: When you choose this path, you learn to speak, read, and write Giant or one other language of your choice if you already know Giant. Additionally, you learn a cantrip of your choice: either {@spell druidcraft|XPHB} or {@spell thaumaturgy|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

features.name: Giant's Havoc
features.description: Your rages pull strength from the primal might of giants, transforming you into a hulking force of destruction. While raging, you gain the following benefits:

details.crushingThrow: When you make a successful ranged attack with a thrown weapon using Strength, you can add your Rage Damage bonus to the attack's damage roll.
details.giantStature: Your reach increases by 5 feet, and if you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If there isn't enough room for you to increase your size, your size doesn't change.

features.name: Elemental Cleaver
features.description: Your bond with the elemental might of giants grows, and you learn to infuse weapons with primordial energy. When you enter your rage, you can choose one weapon that you are holding and infuse it with one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, thunder, or lightning. While you wield the infused weapon during your rage, the weapon's damage type changes to the chosen type, it deals an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of the chosen type when it hits, and it gains the thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. If you throw the weapon, it reappears in your hand the instant after it hits or misses a target. The infused weapon's benefits are suppressed while a creature other than you wields it. While raging and holding the infused weapon, you can use a bonus action to change the infused weapon's current damage type to another one from the damage type options above.

features.name: Mighty Impel
features.description: Your connection to giant strength allows you to hurl both allies and enemies on the battlefield. As a bonus action while raging, you can choose one Medium or smaller creature within your reach and move it to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself. An unwilling creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) to avoid the effect. If, at the end of this movement, the thrown creature isn't on a surface or liquid that can support it, the creature falls, taking damage as normal and landing {@condition prone|XPHB}.

features.name: Demiurgic Colossus
features.description: The primordial power of your rage intensifies. When you rage, your reach increases by 10 feet, your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice), and you can use your Mighty Impel to move creatures that are Large or smaller. In addition, the extra damage dealt by your Elemental Cleaver feature increases to {@dice 2d6}.

name: Path of the Primal Spirit
description: Many Barbarians are in tune with the natural world, but few are as mystically intertwined with it as those who walk the Path of the Primal Spirit. These Barbarians forge powerful connections with beasts and nature spirits, inspiring such ethereal beings to manifest and journey on far-flung adventures.

features.name: Primal Companion
features.description: You magically summon a primal spirit that adopts the form of a beast and accompanies you on your adventures. Choose its stat block: {@creature Primal Guardian|XPHB} or {@creature Primal Striker|XPHB}. In addition, choose an environment that will modify the creature's stat block: Land, Sea, or Sky. You also determine the kind of animal it is, choosing a kind appropriate for the stat block. Whatever companion you choose, it bears eldrich markings indicating its otherworldly origin.

details.friendly: The companion is {@variantrule Friendly|XPHB} to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It vanishes if you die.
details.beastInCombat: The companion acts during your turn. It can move and use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. You can also sacrifice one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to command the beast to take the Beast's Strike action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the companion acts on its own and isn't limited to the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.
details.restoringOrReplacing: If the companion has died within the last hour, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch it and expend a use of your Rage. The companion returns to life immediately with all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can summon a different primal companion, which appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. You choose its stat block and appearance. If you already have a beast from this feature, the old one vanishes.

features.name: Shared Rage
features.description: While your Rage is active, your primal companion has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

features.name: Kin to Beasts
features.description: You always have the {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for them.

features.name: Skinrider's Trance
features.description: You take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to enter a trance and choose your primal companion or one Beast currently under the effect of your {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} spell within 60 feet of yourself. For the duration of this trance, you possess the chosen creature. Once you possess a creature's body, you control it. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and senses are replaced by the creature's. You otherwise keep your game statistics. This possession ends if you choose to exit the trance (no action required by you), if the Beast you're possessing is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or if you and the Beast are on different planes of existence. While in this trance, your body falls into a catatonic state. You can't move or take {@variantrule Reactions|XPHB}, and you're unaware of your surroundings. You can remain in the trance for a number of hours up to half your Barbarian level plus your Constitution modifier. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one use of your Rage (no action).

features.name: Shape of the Wild
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can choose a new form for your primal companion, causing it to transform instantaneously. When you cause your primal companion to transform in this way, its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} change to its new {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one use of your Rage (no action required).

name: Path of the Storm Herald
description: All barbarians harbor a fury within. Their rage grants them superior strength, durability, and speed. Barbarians who follow the Path of the Storm Herald learn to transform that rage into a mantle of primal magic, which swirls around them. When in a fury, a barbarian of this path taps into the forces of nature to create powerful magical effects.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Storm Aura
features.description: Starting at 3rd level, you emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through {@variantrule total cover|XPHB}. Your aura has an effect that activates when you enter your rage, and you can activate the effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action. Choose desert, sea, or tundra. Your aura's effect depends on that chosen environment, as detailed below. You can change your environment choice whenever you gain a level in this class.

details.dc: If your aura's effects require a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.
details.desert: When this effect is activated, all other creatures in your aura take 2 fire damage each. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.
details.sea: When this effect is activated, you can choose one other creature you can see in your aura. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. The target takes {@dice 1d6} lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to {@dice 2d6} at 10th level, {@dice 3d6} at 15th level, and {@dice 4d6} at 20th level.
details.tundra: When this effect is activated, each creature of your choice in your aura gains 2 temporary hit points, as icy spirits inure it to suffering. The temporary hit points increase when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.

features.name: Storm Soul
features.description: At 6th level, the storm grants you benefits even when your aura isn't active. The benefits are based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.

details.desert: You gain resistance to fire damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme heat, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, as an action, you can touch a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried by anyone else and set it on fire.
details.sea: You gain resistance to lightning damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme cold, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, you can breathe underwater. You also gain a swimming speed of 30 feet.
details.tundra: You gain resistance to cold damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme cold, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, as an action, you can touch water and turn a 5-foot cube of it into ice, which melts after 1 minute. This action fails if a creature is in the cube.

features.name: Shielding Storm
features.description: At 10th level, you learn to use your mastery of the storm to protect others. Each creature of your choice has the damage resistance you gained from the Storm Soul feature while the creature is in your Storm Aura.

features.name: Raging Storm
features.description: At 14th level, the power of the storm you channel grows mightier, lashing out at your foes. The effect is based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.

details.desert: Immediately after a creature in your aura hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes fire damage equal to half your barbarian level.
details.sea: When you hit a creature in your aura with an attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is knocked {@condition prone|XPHB}, as if struck by a wave.
details.tundra: Whenever the effect of your Storm Aura is activated, you can choose one creature you can see in the aura. That creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn, as magical frost covers it.

name: Path of the Wild Heart
description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Wild Heart view themselves as kin to animals. These Barbarians learn magical means to communicate with animals, and their Rage heightens their connection to animals as it fills them with supernatural might.
shortDescription: Walk in Community with the Animal World

features.name: Animal Speaker
features.description: You can cast the {@spell Beast Sense|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells but only as Rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them.

features.name: Rage of the Wilds
features.description: Your Rage taps into the primal power of animals. Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.

details.bear: While your Rage is active, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.
details.eagle: When you activate your Rage, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} and {@action Dash|XPHB} actions as part of that {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. While your Rage is active, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to take both of those actions.
details.wolf: While your Rage is active, your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Aspect of the Wilds
features.description: You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice.

details.owl: You have {@variantrule Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@variantrule Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.
details.panther: You have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
details.salmon: You have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

features.name: Nature Speaker
features.description: You can cast the {@spell Commune with Nature|XPHB} spell but only as a Ritual. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Power of the Wilds
features.description: Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.

details.falcon: While your Rage is active, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} if you aren't wearing any armor.
details.lion: While your Rage is active, any of your enemies within 5 feet of you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against targets other than you or another Barbarian who has this option active.
details.ram: While your Rage is active, you can cause a Large or smaller creature to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition when you hit it with a melee attack.

name: Wild Magic
description: Many places in the multiverse abound with beauty, intense emotion, and rampant magic; the Feywild, the Upper Planes, and other realms of supernatural power radiate with such forces and can profoundly influence people. As folk of deep feeling, barbarians are especially susceptible to these wild influences, with some barbarians being transformed by the magic. These magic-suffused barbarians walk the Path of the Wild Magic. Elf, tiefling, aasimar, and genasi barbarians often seek this path, eager to manifest the otherworldly magic of their ancestors.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Magic Awareness
features.description: As an action, you can open your awareness to the presence of concentrated magic. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any spell or magic item within 60 feet of you that isn't behind {@variantrule total cover|XPHB}. When you sense a spell, you learn which school of magic it belongs to. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Wild Surge
features.description: The magical energy rolling inside you sometimes erupts from you. When you enter your rage, roll on the Wild Magic table to determine the magical effect produced. If the effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

wildMagicTable.effect: Shadowy tendrils lash around you. Each creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@dice 1d12} necrotic damage. You also gain {@dice 1d12} temporary hit points.
You teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

An intangible spirit, which looks like a {@creature flumph|MM} or a {@creature pixie|MM} (your choice), appears within 5 feet of one creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. At the end of the current turn, the spirit explodes, and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 1d6} force damage. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again, summoning another spirit, on each of your turns as a bonus action.

Magic infuses one weapon of your choice that you are holding. Until your rage ends, the weapon's damage type changes to force, and it gains the light and thrown properties, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. If the weapon leaves your hand, the weapon reappears in your hand at the end of the current turn.
Whenever a creature hits you with an attack roll before your rage ends, that creature takes {@dice 1d6} force damage, as magic lashes out in retribution.
Until your rage ends, you are surrounded by multicolored, protective lights; you gain a +1 bonus to AC, and while within 10 feet of you, your allies gain the same bonus.
Flowers and vines temporarily grow around you. Until your rage ends, the ground within 15 feet of you is {@variantrule difficult terrain|XPHB} for your enemies.

A bolt of light shoots from your chest. Another creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@dice 1d6} radiant damage and be {@condition blinded|XPHB} until the start of your next turn. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

features.name: Bolstering Magic
features.description: You can harness your wild magic to bolster yourself or a companion. As an action, you can touch one creature (which can be yourself) and confer one of the following benefits of your choice to that creature.

details.attackBonus: For 10 minutes, the creature can roll a {@dice d3} whenever making an attack roll or an ability check and add the number rolled to the {@dice d20} roll.
details.spellSlot: Roll a {@dice d3}. The creature regains one expended spell slot, the level of which equals the number rolled or lower (the creature's choice). Once a creature receives this benefit, that creature can't receive it again until after a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Unstable Backlash
features.description: When you are imperiled during your rage, the magic within you can lash out; immediately after you take damage or fail a saving throw while raging, you can use your reaction to roll on the Wild Magic table and immediately produce the effect rolled. This effect replaces your current Wild Magic effect.

features.name: Controlled Surge
features.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to unleash. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

name: Path of the World Tree
description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree connect with the cosmic tree Yggdrasil through their Rage. This tree grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and the Material Plane. These Barbarians draw on the tree's magic for vitality and as a means of dimensional travel.
shortDescription: Trace the Roots and Branches of the Multiverse

features.name: Vitality of the Tree
features.description: Your Rage taps into the life force of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits.

details.vitalitySurge: When you activate your Rage, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Barbarian level.
details.lifeGivingForce: At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of yourself to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. To determine the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. If any of those {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.

features.name: Branches of the Tree
features.description: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. After the target teleports, you can reduce its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Battering Roots
features.description: During your turn, your reach is 10 feet greater with any {@variantrule Melee|XPHB} weapon that has the {@variantrule Heavy|XPHB} or {@variantrule Versatile|XPHB} property, as tendrils of the World Tree extend from you. When you hit with such a weapon on your turn, you can activate the {@variantrule Push|XPHB} or {@variantrule Topple|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon.

features.name: Travel Along the Tree
features.description: You activate your Rage and as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} while your Rage is active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. In addition, once per Rage, you can also bring up to six willing creatures who are within 10 feet of you. Each creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of your destination space.

name: Path of the Wrathful Dead
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Wrathful Dead commune with the spirits of the deceased and channel their spectral powers. These Barbarians are often preoccupied with the dead whispering in their ears, which urge them to act upon the whims and emotions of the haunting spirits. Some who walk this path learn to master the powers gifted to them; others find they've become servants to the unending chorus clamoring for their attention.
shortDescription: Channel the Rage of the Unquiet Dead

features.name: Rage of the Dead
features.description: Your Rage taps into the endless fury of the unquiet dead. While your Rage is active, you take on aspects of restless spirits and you gain the following benefits.

details.shadowForm: You ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. In addition, you can move through the space of any creature, but you can't end your move in an occupied space.
details.shadowySidestep: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.
details.spectralSight: You see creatures and objects within 120 feet that have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition as if they were visible, and you can see into the Ethereal Plane.

features.name: Final Night Catharsis
features.description: You are overcome by an emotion a nearby spirit experienced at its death. You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice.

details.hate: While your Rage is active, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
details.jealousy: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral assailants to {@condition Grapple|XPHB} the creature. The creature makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failure, the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition until the end of its turn. On a successful save, the creature is not {@condition Grappled|XPHB}; however, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot for that creature until the end of its turn.
details.terror: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make that creature terrified until the start of its next turn. A terrified creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

features.name: Dark Doom Revisited
features.description: Once per active Rage, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to channel a traumatic death. When you do, choose one of the following options.

details.contamination: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Poison damage and gain the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. To determine the Poison damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. An affected creature can use an action to end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on itself.
details.hypothermia: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Cold damage and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved for 1 minute. To determine the Cold damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. A creature can use an action to end this effect on itself.
details.immolation: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Fire damage. To determine the Fire damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. A creature can extinguish the fire on itself by giving itself the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and rolling on the ground. The fire also goes out if it is doused, submerged, or suffocated.

features.name: Death Is but a Door
features.description: Your familiarity with death has left you resistant to its call. You gain the following benefits.

details.hardToKill: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB}. In addition, you must fail four {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB} to die instead of three as normal.
details.returnTheSpirit: You can call the spirits of the deceased to restore life essence to a nearby creature. You can cast the {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}, or {@spell Revivify|XPHB} spell without providing Material components. When you do, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level for {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, 2 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels for {@spell Revivify|XPHB}, and 3 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels for {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this feature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend two uses of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Powered by Pathos
features.description: Your Rage is empowered by the overwhelming emotions of the unquiet dead. Your Final Night Catharsis feature grants an additional effect based on the chosen emotion.

details.hate: While your Rage is active, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
details.jealousy: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral assailants to {@condition Grapple|XPHB} the creature. The creature makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failure, the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition until the end of its turn. On a successful save, the creature is not {@condition Grappled|XPHB}; however, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot for that creature until the end of its turn.
details.terror: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make that creature terrified until the start of its next turn. A terrified creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

name: Path of the Zealot
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Zealot receive boons from a god or pantheon. These Barbarians experience their Rage as an ecstatic episode of divine union that infuses them with power. They are often allies to the priests and other followers of their god or pantheon.
shortDescription: Rage in Ecstatic Union with a God

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: You can channel divine power into your strikes. On each of your turns while your Rage is active, the first creature you hit with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} takes extra damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus half your Barbarian level (round down). The extra damage is Necrotic or Radiant; you choose the type each time you deal the damage.

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: A divine entity helps ensure you can continue the fight. You have a pool of four {@dice d12}s that you can spend to heal yourself. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend dice from the pool, roll them, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The pool's maximum number of dice increases by one when you reach Barbarian levels 6 (5 dice), 12 (6 dice), and 17 (7 dice).

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: Once per active Rage, if you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and you must use the new roll.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a use of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Rage of the Gods
features.description: When you activate your Rage, you can assume the form of a divine warrior. This form lasts for 1 minute or until you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While in this form, you gain the benefits below.

details.flight: You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hover.
details.resistance: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.
details.revivification: When a creature within 30 feet of you would drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend a use of your Rage to instead change the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a number equal to your Barbarian level.

name: College of Adventurers
description: Bards of the College of Adventurers learn from heroes of old and stories of legend. Bards are jacks of all trades, and, for the College of Adventurers, this statement rings even truer. They combine all the skills of their companions into one, allowing them to be versatile and supportive. While not having a direct goal, many adventurer-studying Bards live to tell tales of other heroes or seek to create their own. Stories of great deeds, of desperate cunning, of magical anomalies, or of godly might fuel these Bards.

features.name: Talented Adventurer
features.description: You learn an adventurer's talent of your choice from the "Adventurer's Talent Options" section later in this subclass's description. You learn an additional adventurer's talent of your choice when you reach Bard levels 6 and 14.

details.barbarian: You've learned to fight with primal ferocity. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} you aren't wearing Heavy armor, you can enter a rampage for 1 minute. During this time, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you can't maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}. Your rampage ends early if you use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. Once you use this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.cleric: You've gained the ability to channel divine energy directly from the gods, emboldening allies and sanctifying their attacks. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature within 30 feet of you. For 10 minutes, the target gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Bard level plus your Charisma modifier. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by this talent, it can choose to lose all remaining {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} when it hits a creature with an attack made with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, dealing extra Radiant damage equal to the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} lost. Once you use this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.druid: You can cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. When you cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell with this talent, you can target yourself only and the maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of the Beast you can transform into equals half your Druid level. Once you cast the spell using this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.fighter: You gain proficiency with {@item martial weapons|XPHB} and training with {@item Shields|XPHB}. In addition, you gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice.
details.monk: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}, and you can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. In addition, after you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action or take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make one attack with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
details.paladin: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend a spell slot to deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage to the target, plus another {@dice 1d6} per level of the spell slot. The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} if the target is a Fiend or an Undead. Once you use this talent, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.
details.ranger: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast this spell using a 5th-level spell slot without expending a spell slot. Once you cast the spell using this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.rogue: You've adopted the quick thinking of heroes who have outsmarted their foes. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}: {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB}.
details.sorcerer: Choose one of the following Metamagic Options available to the Sorcerer class: {@variantrule Careful Spell|XPHB}, {@variantrule Distant Spell|XPHB}, {@variantrule Empowered Spell|XPHB}, {@variantrule Extended Spell|XPHB}, {@variantrule Seeking Spell|XPHB}, {@variantrule Subtle Spell|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Transmuted Spell|XPHB}. When you cast a Bard spell, you can apply that Metamagic Option as if you had paid its Sorcery Point cost. Once you use this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.warlock: You learn one {@variantrule Eldritch Invocation|XPHB} of your choice. If an invocation has a level requirement, use your Bard level. When you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the invocations you know and replace it with another invocation for which you qualify.
details.wizard: You prepare any two spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list} that are of a level you can cast. These count as Bard spells for you but do not count against your number of Prepared Spells. When you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of the Wizard spells you have prepared and replace it with another spell from the {@filter Wizard spell list} of a level you can cast.

features.name: Party Planner
features.description: While a creature has a Bardic Inspiration die from you, it can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to take the {@action Help|XPHB} action.

features.name: Well-Rounded
features.description: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice. In addition, you learn one additional adventurer's talent from the Talented Adventurer feature.

features.name: Improvisational Talent
features.description: You can change your adventurer's talent on the fly. When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one of your adventurer's talents with another one from the Talented Adventurer feature. In addition, you learn one additional adventurer's talent.

name: College of Creation
description: Bards believe the cosmos is a work of art—the creation of the first dragons and gods. That creative work included harmonics that continue to resound through existence today, a power known as the Song of Creation.
