You can use this special reaction only to make an opportunity attack, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

name: Champion
description: A Champion focuses on the development of martial prowess in a relentless pursuit of victory. Champions combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows, withstand peril, and garner glory. Whether in athletic contests or bloody battle, Champions strive for the crown of the victor.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: Thanks to your athleticism, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls and Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks. In addition, immediately after you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you can move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You gain another {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice.

features.name: Heroic Warrior
features.description: The thrill of battle drives you toward victory. During combat, you can give yourself {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} whenever you start your turn without it.

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} can now score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 18–20 on the {@dice d20}.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: You attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle, giving you these benefits.

details.defyDeath: {@b Defy Death.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Death Saving Throws. Moreover, when you roll 18–20 on a Death Saving Throw, you gain the benefit of rolling a 20 on it.
details.heroicRally: {@b Heroic Rally.} At the start of each of your turns, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 5 plus your Constitution modifier if you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} and have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB}.

name: Echo Knight
description: A mysterious and feared frontline warrior of the Kryn Dynasty, the Echo Knight has mastered the art of using dunamis to summon the fading shades of unrealized timelines to aid them in battle. Surrounded by echoes of their own might, they charge into the fray as a cycling swarm of shadows and strikes.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Manifest Echo
features.description: 3rd-level Echo Knight feature. You can use a bonus action to magically manifest an echo of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of you. This echo is a magical, translucent, gray image of you that lasts until it is destroyed, until you dismiss it as a bonus action, until you manifest another echo, or until you're {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}. Your echo has AC 14 + your proficiency bonus, 1 hit point, and immunity to all conditions. If it has to make a saving throw, it uses your saving throw bonus for the roll. It is the same size as you, and it occupies its space. On your turn, you can mentally command the echo to move up to 30 feet in any direction (no action required). If your echo is ever more than 30 feet from you at the end of your turn, it is destroyed.

details.teleport: As a bonus action, you can teleport, magically swapping places with your echo at a cost of 15 feet of your movement, regardless of the distance between the two of you.
details.attackFromEcho: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, any attack you make with that action can originate from your space or the echo's space. You make this choice for each attack.
details.opportunityAttack: When a creature that you can see within 5 feet of your echo moves at least 5 feet away from it, you can use your reaction to make an opportunity attack against that creature as if you were in the echo's space.

features.name: Unleash Incarnation
features.description: You can heighten your echo's fury. Whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make one additional melee attack from the echo's position. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Echo Avatar
features.description: You can temporarily transfer your consciousness to your echo. As an action, you can see through your echo's eyes and hear through its ears. During this time, you are {@condition deafened|XPHB} and {@condition blinded|XPHB}. You can sustain this effect for up to 10 minutes, and you can end it at any time (requires no action). While your echo is being used in this way, it can be up to 1,000 feet away from you without being destroyed.

features.name: Shadow Martyr
features.description: You can make your echo throw itself in front of an attack directed at another creature that you can see. Before the attack roll is made, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport the echo to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the targeted creature. The attack roll that triggered the reaction is instead made against your echo. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Reclaim Potential
features.description: You've learned to absorb the fleeting magic of your echo. When an echo of yours is destroyed by taking damage, you can gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} + your Constitution modifier, provided you don't already have temporary hit points. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Legion of One
features.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to create two echoes with your Manifest Echo feature, and these echoes can coexist. If you try to create a third echo, the previous two echoes are destroyed. Anything you can do from one echo's position can be done from the other's instead. In addition, when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of your Unleash Incarnation feature left, you regain one use of that feature.

name: Eldritch Knight
description: Eldritch Knights combine the martial mastery common to all Fighters with a careful study of magic. Their spells both complement and extend their combat skills, providing additional protection to shore up their armor and also allowing them to engage many foes at once with explosive magic.
shortDescription: Support Combat Skills with Arcane Magic

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules as an Eldritch Knight.

details.cantrips: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list}. {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB} and {@spell Shocking Grasp|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list}. When you reach Fighter level 10, you learn another {@filter Wizard|XPHB} cantrip of your choice.
details.spellSlots: The Eldritch Knight Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list}. {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB}, {@spell Jump|XPHB}, and {@spell Shield|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Fighter levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Eldritch Knight Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 7 Fighter, your list of prepared spells can include five Wizard spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace one spell on your list with another {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spell for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spells.

features.name: War Bond
features.description: You learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You perform the ritual over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}. The weapon must be within your reach throughout the ritual, at the conclusion of which you touch the weapon and forge the bond. Once you have bonded a weapon to yourself, you can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. If it is on the same plane of existence, you can summon that weapon as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, causing it to teleport instantly to your hand. You can have up to two bonded weapons, but you can summon only one at a time with a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. If you attempt to bond with a third weapon, you must break the bond with one of the other two.

features.name: War Magic
features.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of one of your {@filter Wizard|XPHB} cantrips that has a casting time of an action.

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: You learn how to make your weapon strikes undercut a creature's ability to withstand your spells. When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: When you use your Action Surge, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can teleport before or after the additional action.

features.name: Improved War Magic
features.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of one of your level 1 or level 2 {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spells that has a casting time of an action.

name: Living Crucible
description: Fighters who become Living Crucibles have discovered an external source of power. While most Fighters train in martial traditions or study the art of war, Living Crucibles hone their craft of alchemy to endure compounds poisonous to others. In exchange for this rigorous physical and mental preparation, these Fighters are able to temporarily push their bodies past their natural limits. Under the influence of their alchemical compounds, these warriors can see in darkness, enhance their speed, inure themselves to magical attacks, and more.
shortDescription: Enhance Your Martial Prowess with Alchemy

features.name: Compound Creator
features.description: You learn to create alchemical compounds toxic to others but empowering to you.

details.compounds: {@b Compounds.} You learn three compounds of your choice from the "Compound Options" section below. You learn two additional compounds of your choice when you reach Fighter levels 7, 10, and 15. Each time you learn new compounds, you can also replace one compound you know with a different one.
details.creating: {@b Creating.} Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} while holding {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, you can use that tool to magically produce any number of compounds. The compound appears in a vial, and the vial vanishes when the compound is consumed or poured out. If any compound remains when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the compound and its vial vanish.
details.consuming: {@b Consuming.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can consume a compound. You can consume a number of compounds up to your Constitution modifier (minimum of one). Once you reach this limit, you cannot benefit from more compounds until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can benefit from multiple compounds at the same time, but consuming multiple vials of the same compound provides no additional effects. Only you can benefit from your compounds. Any other creature that consumes a compound must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Intelligence modifier plus {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

features.name: Student of Alchemy
features.description: Your long nights of vigilance have left your senses attuned to signs of danger, granting you the following benefits. You gain {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, and you gain proficiency with it. In addition, your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} is doubled for ability checks with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}.

features.name: Living Cauldron
features.description: The number of compounds you can safely consume increases to three plus your Constitution modifier (minimum one). At Fighter level 18, the number of compounds you can safely consume increases to five plus your Constitution modifier (minimum one).

features.name: Rapid Consumption
features.description: When you use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to drink a compound, you can drink a second compound.

features.name: Toxin Transmutation
features.description: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage. Also, as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on yourself. When you end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on yourself in this way, you can choose to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Fighter level. You regain the ability to gain these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} after completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Living Catalyst
features.description: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one compound you know with another one.

name: Nightwatcher
description: Nightwatchers come from humble origins, often toiling as guards or investigators. Undaunted by dangers in the darkness, they stand firm against the growing night. Ever vigilant, they use the darkness to surprise and overwhelm foes much greater than themselves.

features.name: Skilled Guardian
features.description: Unraveling mysteries under the cover of night has honed your skills. You gain the following benefits.

details.expertise: {@b Expertise.} Choose one of your skill proficiencies with which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill.
details.skilled: {@b Skilled.} You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice from the following list: {@skill Deception}, {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Perception}, or {@skill Stealth}.

features.name: Ever Vigilant
features.description: Your long nights of vigilance have left your senses attuned to signs of danger, granting you the following benefits.

details.darkvision: {@b Darkvision.} You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} when you gain this feature, its range increases by 60 feet.
details.keenSenses: {@b Keen Senses.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks.
details.warningShout: {@b Warning Shout.} When you make an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to warn creatures of your choice within 30 feet of yourself that can see or hear you. Each creature can then take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll and move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Size Up
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you assess a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. Until the start of your next turn, when that creature makes an attack roll against you or another creature within 5 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll and give {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage.

features.name: Night Stalker
features.description: You are adept at tracking down enemies in the dead of night, giving you these benefits.

details.blindsight: {@b Blindsight.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet for 10 minutes. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.slippery: {@b Slippery.} {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you. While entirely within {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, your movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

features.name: Ready for Action
features.description: When you make an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10. When you roll 18–20 on an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can take one additional action, except the {@action Magic|XPHB} action, on your first turn.

features.name: Beat Down
features.description: Immediately after you miss a creature under the effect of your Size Up feature with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack against that creature if it's within range. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the new attack roll against that creature.

name: Psi Warrior
description: Psi Warriors are Fighters who augment their physical might with psi-infused weapon strikes, telekinetic lashes, and barriers of mental force. Many githyanki train to become such combatants, as do some of the most disciplined high elves. In the world of Eberron, many young kalashtar dream of mastering their innate psionic talent and joining the Psi Warriors. As a Psi Warrior, you hone your psionic abilities alongside your combat skills, using each to enhance your fighting prowess.
shortDescription: Augment Physical Might with Psionic Power

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. It is represented by your Psionic Energy Dice, which fuel powers you have from this subclass. The Psi Warrior Energy Dice table shows the die size and number of these dice you have when you reach certain Fighter levels. Any features in this subclass that use a Psionic Energy Die use only the dice from this subclass. Some of your powers expend the Psionic Energy Die, as specified in a power's description, and you can't use a power if it requires you to use a die when all your Psionic Energy Dice are expended. You regain one of your expended Psionic Energy Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all of them when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

details.protectiveField: {@b Protective Field.} When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend one Psionic Energy Die, roll the die, and reduce the damage taken by the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum reduction of 1), as you create a momentary shield of telekinetic force.
details.psionicStrike: {@b Psionic Strike.} You can propel your weapons with psionic force. Once on each of your turns, immediately after you hit a target within 30 feet of you with an attack and deal damage to it with a weapon, you can expend one Psionic Energy Die, rolling it and dealing force damage to the target equal to the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier.
details.telekineticMovement: {@b Telekinetic Movement.} You can move an object or a creature with your mind. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you target one loose object that is Large or smaller or one willing creature, other than yourself. If you can see the target and it is within 30 feet of you, you can move it up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, if it is a Tiny object, you can move it to or from your hand. Either way, you can move the target horizontally, vertically, or both. Once you take this action, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again.

features.name: Telekinetic Adept
features.description: You have mastered new ways to use your telekinetic abilities, detailed below.

details.psiPoweredLeap: {@b Psi-Powered Leap.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain a Fly Speed equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} until the end of the current turn. Once you take this Bonus Action, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again.
details.telekineticThrust: {@b Telekinetic Thrust.} When you deal damage to a target with your Psionic Strike, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Intelligence modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, you can knock the target {@condition Prone|XPHB} or move it up to 10 feet in any direction horizontally.

features.name: Guarded Mind
features.description: The psionic energy flowing through you has bolstered your mind. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage. Moreover, if you start your turn with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can expend a Psionic Energy Die and end every effect on yourself subjected to those conditions.

features.name: Bulwark of Force
features.description: You can shield yourself and others with telekinetic force. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can choose creatures, which can include yourself, that you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of the chosen creatures has {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to use it again.

features.name: Telekinetic Master
features.description: You always have the {@spell Telekinesis|XPHB} spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast it without a spell slot or components, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence. On each of your turns while you maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on it, including the turn when you cast it, you can make one attack with a weapon as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die (no action required) to restore your use of it.

name: Rune Knight
description: Rune Knights enhance their martial prowess using the supernatural power of runes, an ancient practice that originated with giants. Rune cutters can be found among any family of giants, and you likely learned your methods first or second hand from such a mystical artisan. Whether you found the giant's cave scratched with strange markings, were ## trained by a sage who had studied giant runecraft, or met the giant yourself, you studied the giant's craft and learned how to apply the magic of the runes to empower your equipment.
shortDescription: Enhance Equipment with Giant Rune Magic
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, and you learn to speak, read, and write Giant.

features.name: Rune Carver
features.description: You can use magic runes to enhance your gear. You learn two runes of your choice, from among the runes described below, and each time you gain a level in this class, you can replace one rune you know with a different one from this feature. When you reach certain levels in this class, you learn additional runes, as shown in the Runes Known table. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can touch a number of objects equal to the number of runes you know, and you inscribe a different rune onto each of the objects. To be eligible, an object must be a weapon, a suit of armor, a shield, a piece of jewelry, or something else you can wear or hold in a hand. Your rune remains on an object until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and an object can bear only one of your runes at a time.

details.cloudRune: {@b Cloud Rune.} This rune emulates the deceptive magic used by some cloud giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) checks and Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks. In addition, when you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to invoke the rune and choose a different creature within 30 feet of you, other than the attacker. The chosen creature becomes the target of the attack, using the same roll. This magic can transfer the attack's effects regardless of the attack's range. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.fireRune: {@b Fire Rune.} This rune's magic channels the masterful craftsmanship of great smiths. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses a tool proficiency. In addition, when you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, you can invoke the rune to summon fiery shackles: the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} fire damage, and it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained|XPHB} for 1 minute. While {@condition restrained|XPHB} by the shackles, the target takes {@damage 2d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, banishing the shackles on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.frostRune: {@b Frost Rune.} This rune's magic evokes the might of those who survive in the wintry wilderness, such as frost giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks and Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks. In addition, you can invoke the rune as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to increase your sturdiness. For 10 minutes, you gain a +2 bonus to all ability checks and saving throws that use Strength or Constitution. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.stoneRune: {@b Stone Rune.} This rune's magic channels the judiciousness associated with stone giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks, and you have {@sense darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 120 feet. In addition, when a creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to invoke the rune and force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. Unless the save succeeds, the creature is {@condition charmed|XPHB} by you for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed|XPHB} in this way, the creature has a speed of 0 and is {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}, descending into a dreamy stupor. The creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.hillRune: {@b Hill Rune (Level 7+).} This rune's magic bestows a resilience reminiscent of a hill giant. While wearing or carrying an object that bears this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to poison damage. In addition, you can invoke the rune as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, gaining resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage for 1 minute. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.stormRune: {@b Storm Rune (Level 7+).} Using this rune, you can glimpse the future like a storm giant seer. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks, and you can't be {@condition surprised|XPHB} as long as you aren't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}. In addition, you can invoke the rune as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to enter a prophetic state for 1 minute or until you're {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}. Until the state ends, when you or another creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, a saving throw, or an ability check, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause the roll to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} or {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Giant's Might
features.description: You have learned how to imbue yourself with the might of giants. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you magically gain the following benefits, which last for 1 minute: If you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If you lack the room to become Large, your size doesn't change. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. Once on each of your turns, one of your attacks with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage to a target on a hit. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Runic Shield
features.description: You learn to invoke your rune magic to protect your allies. When another creature you can see within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to reroll the {@dice d20} and use the new roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Great Stature
features.description: The magic of your runes permanently alters you. When you gain this feature, roll {@dice 3d4}. You grow a number of inches in height equal to the roll. Moreover, the extra damage you deal with your Giant's Might feature increases to {@damage 1d8}.

features.name: Master of Runes
features.description: You can invoke each rune you know from your Rune Carver feature twice, rather than once, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Runic Juggernaut
features.description: You learn how to amplify your rune-powered transformation. As a result, the extra damage you deal with the Giant's Might feature increases to {@damage 1d10}. Moreover, when you use that feature, your size can increase to Huge, and while you are that size, your reach increases by 5 feet.

name: Samurai
description: The Samurai is a fighter who draws on an implacable fighting spirit to overcome enemies. A Samurai's resolve is nearly unbreakable, and the enemies in a Samurai's path have two choices: yield or die fighting.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

features.name: Fighting Spirit
features.description: Your intensity in battle can shield you and help you strike true. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on weapon attack rolls until the end of the current turn. When you do so, you also gain 5 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. The number of temporary hit points increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 10 at 10th level and 15 at 15th level. You can use this feature three times, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Elegant Courtier
features.description: Your discipline and attention to detail allow you to excel in social situations. Whenever you make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier. Your self-control also causes you to gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

features.name: Tireless Spirit
features.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of Fighting Spirit remaining, you regain one use.

features.name: Rapid Strike
features.description: You learn to trade accuracy for swift strikes. If you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll against one of the targets, you can forgo the advantage for that roll to make an additional weapon attack against that target, as part of the same action. You can do so no more than once per turn.

features.name: Strength Before Death
features.description: Your fighting spirit can delay the grasp of death. If you take damage that reduces you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and doesn't kill you outright, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to delay falling {@condition unconscious|XPHB}, and you can immediately take an extra turn, interrupting the current turn. While you have 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} during that extra turn, taking damage causes death saving throw failures as normal, and three death saving throw failures can still kill you. When the extra turn ends, you fall {@condition unconscious|XPHB} if you still have 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Deadeye
description: A well-placed bullet is more powerful than a sword, arrow, or spell. Indeed, you believe that every violent conflict should sound like a single loud crack followed by a long silence. Such shots demand perfection, even at range, for when they are done right, they are as deadly for the target as they are stupendous for the audience.

features.name: Eagle Eye
features.description: [Maneuver] Once per turn when you miss with a ranged attack roll, you can expend one Risk Die and add it to the attack roll, potentially causing the attack to hit.

features.name: Sharpshooter's Stance
features.description: You have trained to fire from a stable {@condition Prone|XPHB} position, granting the following conditions.

details.fireWhileProne: You don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ranged attack rolls as a result of the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
details.quickStand: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you can right yourself and thereby end the condition with only 5 feet of movement.

features.name: Concealed Position
features.description: You excel at firing from concealment, granting you the following benefits.

details.camouflage: You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action even if you aren't {@condition Heavily Obscured|XPHB} or behind {@variantrule Three-Quarters Cover|XPHB} or {@variantrule Total Cover|XPHB}, as long as you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. The {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition of this {@action Hide|XPHB} action ends if you don't have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
details.snipersNest: If you make an attack roll while hidden and the roll misses, making the attack roll doesn't reveal your location.

features.name: Reposition
features.description: Whenever a creature misses you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to end the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition on yourself and move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

features.name: Focused Shot
features.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can choose to make only one ranged attack roll using a weapon to make a Focused Shot. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on this attack roll and, on a hit, score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}.

name: High Roller
description: Fortune is a fickle thing—unless you're a High Roller. These Gunslingers are master card sharps and dice throwers that mix their love of risk with their talent for gunplay. High Rollers push their luck until it runs out, then push harder. Why settle for a win when you could bet it all and win big?

features.name: Poker Face
features.description: You gain proficiency with all {@item Gaming Sets|XPHB} and in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Insight}, or {@skill Perception}.

features.name: Liar's Dice
features.description: [Maneuver] When you make a damage roll with a Ranged weapon, you can expend one Risk Die as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} and declare it to be a hidden roll. Roll the damage in secret and declare any total you wish. The GM has the option to call your bluff, in which case you reveal the damage dice you rolled. This has different consequences based on whether or not you lied.

details.gmCallsBluffYouLied: The damage you deal is halved.
details.gmDoesntCallBluff: Use the total damage you declared, even if you rolled a different total.

features.name: Risky Business
features.description: Once per turn when you roll an attack roll against an enemy and the roll doesn't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, you can choose to make the roll with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. When you do, you regain one expended Risk Die.

features.name: Risk Taker
features.description: You can use your Maverick Spirit and Skin of Your Teeth maneuvers without expending a Risk Die. When you do so, roll a {@dice d6} instead of a Risk Die.

features.name: Double or Nothing
features.description: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} using a Ranged weapon, you can gamble for a higher result. Roll a {@dice d20}. If the roll is a 10 or higher, roll all of the attack's damage dice four times and add them together, instead of only two times as normal for a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. If you roll a 9 or lower on the {@dice d20}, the {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} becomes a normal hit.

name: Secret Agent
description: Knowledge is power. The best way to defeat your enemies is by stealing what they know and replacing it with misinformation. To that end, you have been trained in the ways of covert warfare, giving you a broad range of abilities to complement your fearsome gunnery skills.

features.name: Operative Training
features.description: Your covert training grants you the following benefits.

details.concealedShot: You learn the {@spell Concealed Shot|GC:VSS'24} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this cantrip (choose when you select this subclass).
details.operativeTools: You gain a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB} and {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, and you have proficiency with them.
details.skillProficiencies: You gain proficiency in two of these skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Persuasion}, {@skill Sleight of Hand}, or {@skill Stealth}.

features.name: Parting Shot
features.description: [Maneuver] When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Dodge|XPHB} action on your turn, you can expend one Risk Die to make a ranged attack using a weapon as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Add the Risk Die to the damage roll on a hit.

features.name: Fieldcraft
features.description: Your experience in the field grants you the following benefits.

details.quickChange: Using a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, you can create a Costume and don it as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
details.slickTalker: Whenever you make a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) or Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

features.name: Exit Strategy
features.description: When you take damage, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to evade further harm. You have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn, and you can immediately move up to 10 feet. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one Risk Die (no action required).

features.name: License to Kill
features.description: Whenever you deal damage with a Ranged weapon, you can expend either one or two Risk Dice and add them to the damage roll. If you roll the highest number on a Risk Die, you can roll the die again and add it to the damage without expending it, rolling again if it is the highest number again, and so on. The maximum number of Risk Dice you can add to the damage equals your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

name: Spellslinger
description: Magic and guns aren't so different—arcane power is like gunpowder, a spell is like a bullet, and you are like a gun, directing your spells with precision at unfortunate targets. Spellslingers mix the disciplines of gunplay and spellcasting, sometimes loading arcane charges with your shots and firing bullets enhanced with lighting, fire, or flame.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You complement your bullets with the ability to cast spells.

details.cantrips: You know two cantrips of your choice from the Wizard spell list (see that class's section for its list). {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB} and {@spell Message|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Gunslinger level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the Wizard spell list. When you reach Gunslinger level 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
details.spellSlots: The Spellslinger Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 spells from the Wizard spell list. {@spell Chromatic Orb|XPHB}, {@spell Jump|XPHB}, and {@spell Shield|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Gunslinger levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Spellslinger Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the Wizard spell list until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 7 Gunslinger, your list of prepared spells can include five Wizard spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you gain a Gunslinger level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Wizard spell for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Gunslinger spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} or a Ranged weapon as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Wizard spells.

features.name: Bang, You're Dead!
features.description: You can use magic in place of guns.

details.fingerGuns: You learn the {@spell Finger Guns|GC:VSS'24} cantrip.
details.arcaneShot: When you hit a target with a {@spell Finger Guns|GC:VSS'24} attack, you can expend one Risk Die as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} and add it to the damage roll.

features.name: Spellshot
features.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of one of your Wizard cantrips that has a casting time of an action.

features.name: Counter-Mage
features.description: Your experience in combating spellcasters grants you the following benefits.

details.abrogationRounds: Whenever you make a ranged attack roll, you temporarily disrupt protective magic affecting the target. For the duration of the attack, the effects of spells targeting the creature, such as {@spell Mage Armor|XPHB}, as well as the properties and powers of magic items worn or carried by the creature, are suppressed and don't function. The target of the attack can't take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to cast spells such as {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the attack or damage.
details.antimagicShot: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} and the target is affected by your Gut Shot feature, it also impedes the target's ability to cast spells. While the projectile is lodged in the target, it can't cast spells or take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action. Additionally, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws it makes to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
details.inuredToMagic: When you fail a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice 1d6} and add it to the roll, potentially turning the failure into a success.

features.name: Arcane Barrage
features.description: [Maneuver] You can make a spell attack roll, you can expend one Risk Die as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to substitute the spell attack with a ranged attack with a ranged weapon. Add the Risk Die to the attack roll. On a hit, the attack deals the weapon's normal damage, in addition to the effects of the spell attack roll.

name: Trick Shot
description: Accuracy means different things to different people. For you, true accuracy isn't necessarily in hitting a target on the first shot, but might include hitting the mark after the bullet bounces around a dozen times. Your attacks are just as dangerous if they miss, or even after hitting their mark, as others' are while they're still in the air.

features.name: Creative Trajectory
features.description: You can make your projectiles travel in unexpected ways. Your ranged attacks with weapons ignore {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} and {@variantrule Three-Quarters Cover|XPHB}.

features.name: Ricochet
features.description: [Maneuver] When you miss with a ranged attack using a weapon, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend one Risk Die to reroll the attack and add the Risk Die to the roll. You must use the new roll.

features.name: Fancy Gunplay
features.description: Your flashy weapon tricks grant you the following benefits.

details.gunSpinning: Once per turn when you make a Charisma ({@skill Performance}) check or a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check using one of your Ranged weapons, you can roll a Risk Die and add it to the ability check without expending it.
details.speedLoader: On your turn, you can reload a weapon with {@property Reload|GC:VSS'24} without taking an action or {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

features.name: Deft Deflection
features.description: [Maneuver] You can shoot projectiles out of the air. When an ally within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} and expend one Risk Die to grant that ally the benefit of the Skin of Your Teeth maneuver against that attack. You must be holding a Ranged weapon to use this maneuver.

features.name: Pinball Shot
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a ranged attack using a weapon, you can deflect the projectile at additional targets. Choose a different target within 30 feet of the first and make an attack roll against it. On a hit, you can repeat this attack against a new target within 30 feet until you miss or make a total of five attacks. You can't target the same creature with more than one attack each time you use this feature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending two Risk Dice (no action required).

name: White Hat
description: Some Gunslingers live by a code and expect others to do the same. These Gunslingers, known as White Hats, sometimes serve as officers of the law but never hesitate to do what's right when the law says otherwise. Despite their affinity for deadly weapons, White Hats prefer to keep their friends safe and subdue their enemies nonviolently—a preference their enemies don't often oblige.
shortDescription: Protect Your Allies and Uphold the Law

features.name: Lay Down the Law
features.description: [Maneuver] You can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend one Risk Die to keep an eye out for dangers that threaten your companions. Choose an ally that you can see within 60 feet of you. That ally gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled on the Risk Die. Until the start of your next turn, if the ally is hit by an attack, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to make a ranged attack using a weapon against the attacker.

features.name: Steely-Eyed Aura
features.description: An aura of stoic confidence radiates from you in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB}. You and allies within the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. The aura is inactive while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Reach for the Skies
features.description: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a creature, you call for the target to surrender instead of lodging a projectile in it. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your Maneuver save DC or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} and {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} conditions for 1 minute. These conditions end early if the creature takes any damage, if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or if you die. The creature can repeat the Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the conditions on itself on a success.

features.name: Long Arm of the Law
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a Large or smaller creature with a ranged attack using a weapon, you can hobble the target. The creature can't move on its next turn unless it first takes the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action.

features.name: Gold Star Hero
features.description: Through gunslinging heroism, you gain the following benefits.

details.improvedAura: The range of your Steely-Eyed Aura feature increases to 30 feet.
details.ironCladLaw: When you use your Lay Down the Law maneuver, the ally has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage until the start of your next turn.
details.stunnedSurrender: When a creature fails its saving throw against your Reach for the Skies feature, it has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition instead of the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

name: Way of the Ascendant Dragon
description: The dragon god Bahamut is known to travel the Material Plane in the guise of a young monk, and legend says that he founded the first monastery of the Way of the Ascendant Dragon in this guise. The fundamental teaching of this tradition holds that by emulating dragons, a monk becomes a more integrated part of the world and its magic. By altering their spirit to resonate with draconic might, monks who follow this tradition augment their prowess in battle, bolster their allies, and can even soar through the air on draconic wings. But all this power is in service of a greater goal: achieving a spiritual unity with the essence of the Material Plane.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

entries.origin: You honed your abilities by aligning your spirit with a dragon's world-altering power.
A dragon personally took an active role in shaping your inner energy.
You studied at a monastery that traces its teachings back centuries or more to a single dragon's instruction, or one that is devoted to a dragon god.
You spent long stretches meditating in the region around an ancient dragon's lair, absorbing that lair's ambient magic.
You found a scroll written in Draconic that contained inspiring new techniques.
After a dream featuring a five-handed dragonborn, you awoke with the mystical breath of dragons.

features.name: Draconic Disciple
features.description: You can channel draconic power to magnify your presence and imbue your unarmed strikes with the essence of a dragon's breath. You gain the following benefits.

details.draconicPresence: {@b Draconic Presence.} If you fail a Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) or Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check, you can use your reaction to reroll the check, as you tap into the mighty presence of dragons. Once this feature turns a failure into a success, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
details.draconicStrike: {@b Draconic Strike.} When you damage a target with an unarmed strike, you can change the damage type to acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison.
details.tongueOfDragons: {@b Tongue of Dragons.} You learn to speak, read, and write {@language Draconic} or one other language of your choice.

features.name: Breath of the Dragon
features.description: You can channel destructive waves of energy, like those created by the dragons you emulate. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with an exhalation of draconic energy in either a 20-foot cone or a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide (your choice). Choose a damage type: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw against your ki save DC, taking damage of the chosen type equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. At 11th level, the damage of this feature increases to three rolls of your Martial Arts die. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. While you have no uses available, you can spend 2 ki points to use this feature again.

features.name: Wings Unfurled
features.description: When you use your Step of the Wind, you can unfurl spectral draconic wings from your back that vanish at the end of your turn. While the wings exist, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Aspect of the Wyrm
features.description: The power of your draconic spirit now radiates from you, warding your allies or inspiring fear in your enemies. As a bonus action, you create an aura of draconic power that radiates 10 feet from you for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain one of the following effects of your choice. Once you create this aura, you can't create it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend 3 ki points to create it again.

details.frighteningPresence: {@b Frightful Presence.} When you create this aura, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can choose a creature within the aura. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your ki save DC or become {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a successful save.
details.resistance: {@b Resistance.} Choose a damage type when you activate this aura: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison. You and your allies within the aura have resistance to that damage.

features.name: Ascendant Aspect
features.description: Your draconic spirit reaches its peak. You gain the following benefits.

details.augmentBreath: {@b Augment Breath.} When you use your Breath of the Dragon, you can expend 1 ki point to augment its shape and power. The exhalation of draconic energy becomes either a 60-foot cone or a 90-foot line that is 5 feet wide (your choice), and each creature in that area takes damage equal to four rolls of your Martial Arts die on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
details.blindsight: {@b Blindsight.} You gain {@sense blindsight} out to 10 feet. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn't behind total cover, even if you're blinded or in darkness. Moreover, you can see an invisible creature within that range, unless the creature successfully hides from you.
details.explosiveFury: {@b Explosive Fury.} When you activate your Aspect of the Wyrm, draconic fury explodes from you. Choose any number of creatures you can see in your aura. Each of those creatures must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your ki save DC or take {@damage 3d10} acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (your choice).

name: Way of the Astral Self
description: A monk who follows the Way of the Astral Self believes their body is an illusion. They see their ki as a representation of their true form, an astral self. This astral self has the capacity to be a force of order or disorder, with some monasteries training students to use their power to protect the weak and other instructing aspirants in how to manifest their true selves in service to the mighty.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Arms of the Astral Self
features.description: Your mastery of your ki allows you to summon a portion of your astral self. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 ki point to summon the arms of your astral self. When you do so, each creature of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take force damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die. For 10 minutes, these spectral arms hover near your shoulders or surround your arms (your choice). You determine the arms' appearance, and they vanish early if you are {@condition incapacitated|XPHB} or die. While the spectral arms are present, you gain the following benefits.

details.wisdomForStrength: You can use your Wisdom modifier in place of your Strength modifier when making Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
details.unarmedStrikes: You can use the spectral arms to make unarmed strikes.
details.reach: When you make an unarmed strike with the arms on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.
details.wisdomForAttacks: The unarmed strikes you make with the arms can use your Wisdom modifier in place of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls, and their damage type is force.

features.name: Visage of the Astral Self
features.description: You can summon the visage of your astral self. As a bonus action, or as part of the bonus action you take to activate Arms of the Astral Self, you can spend 1 ki point to summon this visage for 10 minutes. It vanishes early if you are {@condition incapacitated|XPHB} or die. The spectral visage covers your face like a helmet or mask. You determine its appearance. While the spectral visage is present, you gain the following benefits.

details.astralSight: {@b Astral Sight.} You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.
details.wisdomOfTheSpirit: {@b Wisdom of the Spirit.} You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) and Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks.
details.wordOfTheSpirit: {@b Word of the Spirit.} When you speak, you can direct your words to a creature of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of you, making it so only that creature can hear you. Alternatively, you can amplify your voice so that all creatures within 600 feet can hear you.

features.name: Body of the Astral Self
features.description: When you have both your astral arms and visage summoned, you can cause the body of your astral self to appear (no action required). This spectral body covers your physical form like a suit of armor, connecting with the arms and visage. You determine its appearance. While the spectral body is present, you gain the following benefits.

details.deflectEnergy: {@b Deflect Energy.} When you take acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, or thunder damage, you can use your reaction to deflect it. When you do so, the damage you take is reduced by {@dice 1d10} + your Wisdom modifier (minimum reduction of 1).
details.empoweredArms: {@b Empowered Arms.} Once on each of your turns when you hit a target with the Arms of the Astral Self, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your Martial Arts die.

features.name: Awakened Astral Self
features.description: Your connection to your astral self is complete, allowing you to unleash its full potential. As a bonus action, you can spend 5 ki points to summon the arms, visage, and body of your astral self and awaken it for 10 minutes. This awakening ends early if you are {@condition incapacitated|XPHB} or die. While your astral self is awakened, you gain the following benefits.

details.armorOfTheSpirit: {@b Armor of the Spirit.} You gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class.
details.astralBarrage: {@b Astral Barrage.} Whenever you use the Extra Attack feature to attack twice, you can instead attack three times if all the attacks are made with your astral arms.

name: Way of the Drunken Master
description: The Way of the Drunken Master teaches its students to move with the jerky, unpredictable movements of a drunkard. A drunken master sways, tottering on unsteady feet, to present what seems like an incompetent combatant who proves frustrating to engage. The drunken master's erratic stumbles conceal a carefully executed dance of blocks, parries, advances, attacks, and retreats. A drunken master often enjoys playing the fool to bring gladness to the despondent or to demonstrate humility to the arrogant, but when battle is joined, the drunken master can be a maddening, masterful foe.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance} skill if you don't already have it. Your martial arts technique mixes combat training with the precision of a dancer and the antics of a jester. You also gain proficiency with {@item brewer's supplies|XPHB} if you don't already have it.

features.name: Drunken Technique
features.description: At 3rd level, you learn how to twist and turn quickly as part of your Flurry of Blows. Whenever you use Flurry of Blows, you gain the benefit of the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action, and your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Tipsy Sway
features.description: Starting at 6th level, you can move in sudden, swaying ways. You gain the following benefits.

details.leapToYourFeet: {@b Leap to Your Feet.} When you're {@condition prone|XPHB}, you can stand up by spending 5 feet of movement, rather than half your speed.
details.redirectAttack: {@b Redirect Attack.} When a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you can spend 1 ki point as a reaction to cause that attack to hit one creature of your choice, other than the attacker, that you can see within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Drunkard's Luck
features.description: Starting at 11th level, you always seem to get a lucky bounce at the right moment. When you make an ability check, an attack roll, or a saving throw and have {@variantrule disadvantage|XPHB} on the roll, you can spend 2 ki points to cancel the disadvantage for that roll.

features.name: Intoxicated Frenzy
features.description: At 17th level, you gain the ability to make an overwhelming number of attacks against a group of enemies. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can make up to three additional attacks with it (up to a total of five Flurry of Blows attacks), provided that each Flurry of Blows attack targets a different creature this turn.

name: Warrior of the Elements
description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes. Harnessing their supernatural focus, these Monks momentarily tame the energy of the Elemental Chaos to empower themselves in and out of battle.

features.name: Elemental Attunement
features.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to imbue yourself with elemental energy. The energy lasts for 10 minutes or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. You gain the following benefits while this feature is active.

details.reach: {@b Reach.} When you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, your reach is 10 feet greater than normal, as elemental energy extends from you.
details.elementalStrikes: {@b Elemental Strikes.} Whenever you hit with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can cause it to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage rather than its normal damage type. When you deal one of these types with it, you can also force the target to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you can move the target up to 10 feet toward or away from you, as elemental energy swirls around it.

features.name: Elemental Burst
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 2 Focus Points and choose a point within 120 feet of yourself. A burst of elemental energy erupts in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} centered on that point. Each creature in the {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. You choose the damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

features.name: Stride of the Elements
features.description: When you use your Elemental Attunement feature, you also gain a Fly Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for the duration of that feature.

features.name: Elemental Epitome
features.description: When you use your Elemental Attunement feature, you also gain the following benefits for the duration of that feature.

details.damageResistance: {@b Damage Resistance.} You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. At the start of each of your turns, you can change this choice.
details.destructiveStride: {@b Destructive Stride.} When you use your Step of the Wind, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 20 feet until the end of the turn. For that duration, any creature of your choice takes damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you enter a space within 5 feet of it. The damage type is your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.
details.empoweredStrikes: {@b Empowered Strikes.} Once on each of your turns, you can deal extra damage to a target equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you hit it with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. The extra damage is the same type you chose for the Damage Resistance in this feature.

name: Way of the Kensei
description: Monks of the Kensei train relentlessly with their weapons, to the point where the weapon becomes an extension of the body. Founded on a mastery of sword fighting, the tradition has expanded to include many different weapons. A kensei sees a weapon in much the same way a calligrapher or painter regards a pen or brush. Whatever the weapon, the kensei views it as a tool used to express the beauty and precision of the martial arts. That such mastery makes a kensei a peerless warrior is but a side effect of intense devotion, practice, and study.
shortDescription: Master Weapons as Extensions of Self
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Path of the Kensei
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your special martial arts training leads you to master the use of certain weapons. You gain the following benefits.

details.kenseiWeapons: {@b Kensei Weapons.} Choose two types of weapons to be your kensei weapons: one melee weapon and one ranged weapon. Each of these weapons can be any {@filter simple or martial weapon that lacks the heavy and special properties|XPHB}. The longbow is also a valid choice. You gain proficiency with these weapons if you don't already have it. Weapons of the chosen types are monk weapons for you. Many of this tradition's features work only with your kensei weapons. When you reach 6th, 11th, and 17th level in this class, you can choose another type of weapon—either melee or ranged—to be a kensei weapon for you, following the criteria above.
details.agileParry: {@b Agile Parry.} If you make an unarmed strike as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and are holding a kensei weapon, you can use it to defend yourself if it is a melee weapon. You gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, while the weapon is in your hand and you aren't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.
details.kenseiShot: {@b Kensei's Shot.} You can use a bonus action on your turn to make your ranged attacks with a kensei weapon more deadly. When you do so, any target you hit with a ranged attack using a kensei weapon takes an extra {@damage 1d4} damage of the weapon's type. You retain this benefit until the end of the current turn.
details.wayOfTheBrush: {@b Way of the Brush.} You gain proficiency with your choice of {@item calligrapher's supplies|XPHB} or {@item painter's supplies|XPHB}.

features.name: One with the Blade
features.description: At 6th level, you extend your ki into your kensei weapons, granting you the following benefits.

details.magicKenseiWeapons: {@b Magic Kensei Weapons.} Your attacks with your kensei weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
details.deftStrike: {@b Deft Strike.} When you hit a target with a kensei weapon, you can spend 1 ki point to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target equal to your Martial Arts die. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.

features.name: Sharpen the Blade
features.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to augment your weapons further with your ki. As a bonus action, you can expend up to 3 ki points to grant one kensei weapon you touch a bonus to attack and damage rolls when you attack with it. The bonus equals the number of ki points you spent. This bonus lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature again. This feature has no effect on a magic weapon that already has a bonus to attack and damage rolls.

features.name: Unerring Accuracy
features.description: At 17th level, your mastery of weapons grants you extraordinary accuracy. If you miss with an attack roll using a monk weapon on your turn, you can reroll it. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.

name: Warrior of the Leaden Crown
description: In contrast to the esoteric ideals of other orders, Warriors of the Leaden Crown have practical aims: the self-governance of all people. These Monks see history as a series of clashes between groups of powerful beings where Humanoids are at best collateral damage and at worst disposable pawns. To break this cycle of dependence and destruction, Warriors of the Leaden Crown master mental abilities to fight against the otherworldly powers of the multiverse and protect Humanoid sovereignty. One component of this plan involves training to do battle with powerful planar foes. The other equally important component is ensuring that Humanoid societies are prepared to overthrow those already dominating them. To that end, these Monks seek political positions that place them in or near decision-making roles where they can influence others to fight back against Arch Seraphs, Arch Daemons, and Primordials.

features.name: Subtle Hand
features.description: Your martial arts are enhanced by a capacity for telekinetic strikes. During your turn, your reach is 5 feet greater with {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}. In addition, when you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can choose to have it deal your choice of Psychic damage or its normal damage type.

features.name: Psionic Prowess
features.description: Your psychic powers have manifested in the ability to cast certain spells. You know the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} cantrip. You can cast it without Verbal or Somatic components, and you can make the spectral hand {@condition Invisible|XPHB}. In addition, you can cast certain spells by expending Focus Points. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 Focus Point to cast {@spell Detect Evil and Good|XPHB} or {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}. You can also take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 2 Focus Points to cast {@spell Hold Person|XPHB}, {@spell Levitate|XPHB}, or {@spell Shatter|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you can cast them without Material components.

features.name: Unsubtle Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} or Monk weapon, you can force it to make a Strength saving throw against your Focus Point save DC. On a failed save, you can move the target up to 10 feet toward or away from you.

features.name: Psychic Crush
features.description: Each time you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it gains a Pressure Point. A creature loses all Pressure Points if you cause a different creature to gain a Pressure Point or after 1 minute, whichever comes first. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Focus Point to telekinetically crush a creature with 1 or more of your Pressure Points. The creature loses all Pressure Points and must make a Strength saving throw against your Focus Point save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage per Pressure Point, and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

features.name: Psionic Mastery
features.description: After much training, you have mastered the psionic disciplines necessary to defend mortals from planar threats. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can spend 5 Focus Points to cast {@spell Dispel Evil and Good|XPHB}, {@spell Hold Monster|XPHB}, {@spell Telekinesis|XPHB}, or {@spell Wall of Force|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you can cast them without Material components.

name: Way of the Long Death
description: Monks of the Way of the Long Death are obsessed with the meaning and mechanics of dying. They capture creatures and prepare elaborate experiments to capture, record, and understand the moments of their demise. They use this knowledge to guide their understanding of martial arts, yielding a deadly fighting style.
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Touch of Death
features.description: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your study of death allows you to extract vitality from another creature as it nears its demise. When you reduce a creature within 5 feet of you to 0 hit points, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier + your monk level (minimum of 1 temporary hit point).

features.name: Hour of Reaping
features.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to unsettle or terrify those around you as an action, for your soul has been touched by the shadow of death. When you take this action, each creature within 30 feet of you that can see you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened|XPHB} of you until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Mastery of Death
features.description: Beginning at 11th level, you use your familiarity with death to escape its grasp. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can expend 1 ki point (no action required) to have 1 hit point instead.

features.name: Touch of the Long Death
features.description: Starting at 17th level, your touch can channel the energy of death into a creature. As an action, you touch one creature within 5 feet of you, and you expend 1 to 10 ki points. The target must make a Constitution saving throw, and it takes {@damage 2d10} necrotic damage per ki point spent on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

name: Way of Mercy
description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians, but they bring a swift end to their enemies. They often wear masks, presenting themselves as faceless bringers of life and death.
shortDescription: Manipulate Forces of Life and Death

features.name: Hand of Harm
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage, you can expend 1 Focus Point to deal extra Necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Hand of Healing
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to touch a creature and restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.
