When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} with a use of this feature without expending a Focus Point for the healing.

features.name: Implements of Mercy
features.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight} and {@skill Medicine} skills and proficiency with the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}.

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description: Your Hand of Harm and Hand of Healing improve.

details.handOfHarm: {@b Hand of Harm.} When you use Hand of Harm on a creature, you can also give that creature the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
details.handOfHealing: {@b Hand of Healing.} When you use Hand of Healing, you can also end one of the following conditions on the creature you heal: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace each of the {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} with a use of Hand of Healing without expending Focus Points for the healing. In addition, when you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} with Flurry of Blows and deal damage, you can use Hand of Harm with that strike without expending a Focus Point for Hand of Harm. You can still use Hand of Harm only once per turn. You can use these benefits a total number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description: Your mastery of life energy opens the door to the ultimate mercy. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 Focus Points. The creature then returns to life with a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 4d10} plus your Wisdom modifier. If the creature died with any of the following conditions, the creature revives without them: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Way of the Open Hand
description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and channel their own energy to protect themselves from harm.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target.

details.addle: {@b Addle.} The target can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.
details.push: {@b Push.} The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.
details.topple: {@b Topple.} The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB}). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Fleet Step
features.description: When you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you take an action to end them. Alternatively, when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with this action. To use this action, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you use this action, the target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 10d12} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can end the vibrations harmlessly (no action required).

name: Warrior of Pride
description: Monks who become Warriors of Pride value themselves above all. These monks focus on mastering their form, ego, and their destructive power. They display monastic aesthetics publicly with the intention of gaining the respect and admiration of "lesser beings." The prideful traditions of this order include adorning themselves with jewelry, hiding their scars, and commanding respect from those who would oppose them.

features.name: Tall Tales
features.description: You have gained a knack for telling embellished tales of your past achievements. You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

features.name: Bruised Ego
features.description: Your ego strengthens as you fight to prove your value. When you expend a Focus Point, you can also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Point|XPHB}). While you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, you gain twice that amount instead.

features.name: Assertive Attacker
features.description: While you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} and Monk weapons.

features.name: Inescapable Retribution
features.description: Damage dealt to you is damage dealt to your pride, and that is something you simply can't allow. Whenever a creature deals damage to you, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 Focus Point. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Redoubled Efforts
features.description: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} while you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra damage dealt by the attack.

features.name: Ever Prideful
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can expend 1 Focus Point to enter a trance. While in this trance, you have the following effects.

details.unconsciousImmunity: You are immune to the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
details.cantCast: You can't cast or concentrate on spells.
details.deathSaves: You suffer 1 {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure from damage from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} instead of 2.
details.maintenance: Whenever you start your turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you must expend 1 Focus Point to maintain a trance, and you make {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB} as normal.

features.name: Egotistical
features.description: Injuries to your pride enrage you. You are considered {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} if your current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are below your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum.

name: Warrior of Regret
description: No one is born intent on bringing evil into this world, yet many lives are transformed by the suffering inflicted upon them. Warriors of Regret are haunted by the shadows of evil deeds done. Through training and reflection, Warriors of Regret accept their past can't be erased. Instead, they focus on forging remorse and grief into a weapon against their foes. Though their misdeeds follow their every step, Warriors of Regret are not powerless to atone for them.

features.name: Shade of Regret
features.description: Once per turn, on your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point (no action required) to create a shade of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of yourself. The shade is intangible and doesn't occupy its space. It lasts until the end of your next turn, but it ends early if you dismiss it (no action required) or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. While it persists, you gain the following benefits.

details.shadeStrike: {@b Shade Strike.} When you use Flurry of Blows, you can have the attacks originate from the shade instead of from yourself. Attacks originating from the shade deal Necrotic or Force damage (your choice) rather than their normal damage type.
details.move: {@b Move.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the shade up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is within 60 feet of yourself.

features.name: The Road Not Traveled
features.description: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action, instead of moving, you can teleport yourself or an ally within 60 feet of you to the location of your shade.

features.name: Aid Not Given
features.description: You can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to take the Help action or touch a creature and restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier. You can use this feature as though you were in the shade's space.

features.name: Crushing Guilt
features.description: You can expend 3 Focus Points to release your pent-up guilt in a crushing wave that drops your foes to their knees. Creatures of your choice in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you or your shade must make a Wisdom saving throw against your Focus Point save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes Psychic damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

features.name: Relive the Past
features.description: Your Shade of Regret now lasts for 10 minutes. After using Flurry of Blows, your Shade can make one additional {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as per Shade Strike. You can also use the Stunning Strike feature through the Shade Strike.

name: Shadow Magic
description: Warriors of Shadow practice stealth and subterfuge, harnessing the power of the Shadowfell. They are at home in darkness, able to draw gloom around themselves to hide, leap from shadow to shadow, and take on a wraithlike form.
shortDescription: Harness Shadow Power for Stealth and Subterfuge

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: You have learned to draw on the power of the Shadowfell, gaining the following benefits.

details.darkness: {@b Darkness.} You can expend 1 Focus Point to cast the {@spell Darkness|XPHB} spell without spell components. You can see within the spell's area when you cast it with this feature. While the spell persists, you can move its area of {@spell Darkness|XPHB} to a space within 60 feet of yourself at the start of each of your turns.
details.darkvision: {@b Darkvision.} You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.
details.shadowyFigments: {@b Shadowy Figments.} You know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} spell. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: While you are in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. You then have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

features.name: Improved Shadow Step
features.description: You can draw on your Shadowfell connection to empower your teleportation. When you use your Shadow Step, you can expend 1 Focus Point to remove the requirement that you must start and end in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} for that use of the feature. As part of this {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} immediately after you teleport.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while entirely within {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can expend 3 Focus Points to shroud yourself with shadows for 1 minute, until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or until you end your turn in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}. While shrouded by these shadows, you gain the following benefits.

details.invisibility: {@b Invisibility.} You have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.
details.partiallyIncorporeal: {@b Partially Incorporeal.} You can move through occupied spaces as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. If you end your turn in such a space, you are shunted to the last unoccupied space you were in.
details.shadowFlurry: {@b Shadow Flurry.} You can use your Flurry of Blows without expending any Focus Points.

name: Way of the Sun Soul
description: Monks of the Way of the Sun Soul learn to channel their life energy into searing bolts of light. They teach that meditation can unlock the ability to unleash the indomitable light shed by the soul of every living creature.
shortDescription: Channel Life Energy into Radiant Light
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Radiant Sun Bolt
features.description: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can hurl searing bolts of magical radiance. You gain a new attack option that you can use with the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. The special attack is a ranged spell attack with a range of 30 feet. You are proficient with it, and you add your Dexterity modifier to its attack and damage rolls. Its damage is radiant, and its damage die is a {@dice d4}. This die changes as you gain monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk table. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and use this special attack as part of it, you can spend 1 ki point to make the special attack twice as a bonus action. When you gain the Extra Attack feature, this special attack can be used for any of the attacks you make as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action.
features.note: Subclass source: XGE, page 35. Also found in SCAG, page 131.

features.name: Searing Arc Strike
features.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to channel your ki into searing waves of energy. Immediately after you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can spend 2 ki points to cast the {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} spell as a bonus action. You can spend additional ki points to cast {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} as a higher-level spell. Each additional ki point you spend increases the spell's level by 1. The maximum number of ki points (2 plus any additional points) that you can spend on the spell equals half your monk level.

features.name: Searing Sunburst
features.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to create an orb of light that erupts into a devastating explosion. As an action, you magically create an orb and hurl it at a point you choose within 150 feet, where it erupts into a sphere of radiant light for a brief but deadly instant. Each creature in that 20-foot-radius sphere must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} radiant damage. A creature doesn't need to see the orb to be affected. You can increase the sphere's damage by spending ki points. Each point you spend, to a maximum of 3, increases the damage by {@damage 2d6}.

features.name: Sun Shield
features.description: At 17th level, you become wreathed in a luminous, magical aura. You shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. You can extinguish or restore the light as a bonus action. If a creature hits you with a melee attack while this light shines, you can use your reaction to deal radiant damage to the creature. The radiant damage equals 5 + your Wisdom modifier.

name: Carver Guild
description: Since the beginning of time, the brave glared into danger's ferocious eyes and fought monsters. Those who survived were the ones smart enough to arm themselves properly and know precisely where to strike—or those who knew when to flee and return with a new plan. Members of the Carver Guild are the Monster Hunters typically called upon when immediate danger threatens a settlement and there is no army or militia available. Carvers stride unflinchingly toward death, armed with years of training and the knowledge passed down from those before them.

features.name: Equipped for Battle
features.description: You gain training with {@item Heavy armor|XPHB}.

features.name: Close Quarters
features.description: Your skill in close combat enables you to inflict crushing blows while keeping your opponent off balance. When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Melee weapon, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the same type dealt by the weapon. That creature has {@status Disadvantage} on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

features.name: True Grit
features.description: You have {@status Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, and you are Immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition caused by creature types in your Monster Grimoire. Additionally, when you hit a creature with an attack as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can choose a {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature within 60 feet that can see you (including yourself). The condition ends on that creature.

features.name: Terrorize the Terrors
features.description: Your reputation has become such that monsters preying on the fearful have come to fear you. When you hit a creature with an attack as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. The DC for the saving throw equals 8 plus your Intelligence modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

features.name: Deadly Redirect
features.description: Your strikes have become even deadlier. The extra damage of your Close Quarters increases to {@damage 3d6}. In addition, if you deal damage to a creature with Close Quarters, the target has {@status Disadvantage} on all attack rolls until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Controlled Footwork
features.description: You are such an effective combatant that you are always in control and never off balance. You can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} twice in a round instead of once.

name: Devourer Guild
description: Folk in Etharis don't always speak highly of Monster Hunters, and many consider them to be just as depraved and inhuman as the evils they vanquish. Many of the appalling tales about Monster Hunters can be attributed, directly or indirectly, to the Devourer Guild, who are accused of being monstrous cannibals. The truth is barely any better. Devourers have spent their days consuming the flesh and blood of the monsters they slay, and, over time, their metabolism has changed to tolerate this disgusting practice. Devourers adopt mutations shortly after consuming their prey.
shortDescription: Gain Power by Consuming Monster Flesh

features.name: Alchemical Gastronomy
features.description: You gain proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} and {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}.

features.name: Transmuting Metabolism
features.description: You gain the ability to consume monster remains, which causes your body to adopt powerful and frightening mutations. These appear in the "Mutations" section later in the subclass's description.

details.salvagingPortions: {@b Salvaging Portions.} As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can harvest a small portion from the physical remains of a creature. Only one portion can be harvested from each creature. Record the monster's creature type. A harvested portion lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at which point it loses its potency.
details.consumingPortions: {@b Consuming Portions.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can consume a portion. After you consume the portion, you choose a mutation to gain, depending on the creature's type. You can gain the benefits of consumed portions a number of times up to 1 plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1). When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you regain the ability to safely consume portions. You can benefit from multiple portions simultaneously, but you can't gain the same mutation more than once at the same time.

features.name: Mutations
features.description: The mutations are presented here in alphabetical order, with required creature types shown in brackets after the mutation's name.

details.acidBlood: {@b Acid Blood (Aberration, Ooze, Plant).} For 1 minute, your blood becomes acidic. Each time a creature hits you with an attack while within 5 feet of you, it takes {@damage 2d6} Acid damage.
details.acumen: {@b Acumen (Celestial, Fiend, Fey).} For 1 hour, you have {@status Advantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks. Additionally, choose either {@skill Intimidation} or {@skill Persuasion}. You have {@status Advantage} on ability checks using the chosen skill.
details.awareness: {@b Awareness (All).} For 10 minutes, you know the location of creatures that are the same type as the creature you consumed within 60 feet of you that are not behind Total Cover.
details.flight: {@b Flight (Celestial, Dragon, Elemental).} For 10 minutes, you grow feathery, leathery, or spectral wings from your back. You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. You can't benefit from this mutation while wearing {@item Heavy armor|XPHB}.
details.infused: {@b Infused (Dragon, Elemental, Ooze).} Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. For 10 minutes, whenever you hit with an attack, you can cause it to deal the chosen type rather than its normal damage type.
details.invisibility: {@b Invisibility (Fey, Fiend, Undead).} For 10 minutes, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. This effect ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), or you attack or cast a spell.
details.might: {@b Might (Beast, Construct, Giant).} For 1 minute, you have {@status Advantage} on Strength checks and saving throws. You also have {@status Advantage} on attack rolls using Strength, and you deal {@damage 1d6} extra damage to targets you hit with a Strength-based attack. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.
details.naturalWeapons: {@b Natural Weapons (Beast, Dragon, Monstrosity).} For 1 hour, you gain natural weapons. You can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. This damage can be Piercing or Slashing. You choose each time you make an attack. You can also make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}.
details.regeneration: {@b Regeneration (Beast, Giant, Monstrosity).} You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Monster Hunter level.
details.resistance: {@b Resistance (Construct, Elemental, Undead).} For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
details.telepathy: {@b Telepathy (Aberration, Fiend, Plant).} For 1 hour, you can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Your telepathic utterances are in a language you know, and the creature understands you only if it knows that language. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage.

features.name: Synchronized Response
features.description: Your ingestion of monsters heightens your understanding of them and their behaviour. You gain this additional effect when you consume a monster portion: For 1 minute, when you make an attack as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage. This damage has the same type as the weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} used for the attack.

features.name: Gnawing Hunger
features.description: Your hunger for your enemy allows you to partake of its essence during battle. When you deal damage to a creature with a melee attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage dealt. If the target is a creature type in your Monster Grimoire, you instead gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Alchemical Decoctions
features.description: You can spend 1 hour and 20 GP worth of alchemical ingredients (such as special herbs or monster salvage) to use your {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} to convert a monster portion into a decoction. A decoction is a magic potion that grants the benefits of a consumed monster portion. You can have 4 unconsumed decoctions active. You can destroy a decoction as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. A creature other than you can consume 1 decoction without adverse effects. A creature gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level for each decoction it consumes after the first. A creature must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before it regains the ability to safely consume a decoction.

features.name: Acquired Taste
features.description: Your hunger for monster portions has increased to the point of being insatiable. You can now consume 1 additional portion safely. Additionally, for 1 minute when you consume a portion, you have {@status Advantage} on attack rolls made as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

name: Occultist Guild
description: In Etharis, there are those who fear magic, and those who seek to understand it. Monster Hunters from the Occultist Guild know folk from both sides are equally dangerous. An Occultist investigates the arcane, the supernatural, and the uncanny, while avoiding the influence of superstition or ideology. Despite their reputation as mage hunters, Occultists don't burn their enemies at the stake—Occultists put them to the sword, as that's what swords are for. Knowledge and magic give Occultists an edge over their mystical adversaries. Their spells are used to detect and defend against dangerous arcana, and, above all, magic is used to destroy monsters that can't be harmed by steel.

features.name: Acolyte of the Occult
features.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Your study of the occult gives you the ability to cast spells.

details.cantrips: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard}. Whenever you gain a Monster Hunter level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard}. When you reach Monster Hunter level 10, you learn another {@filter Wizard|spells|class=Wizard} cantrip of your choice.
details.spellSlots: The Occultist Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 {@filter Wizard spells|spells|class=Wizard}. {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB}, {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB}, and {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Monster Hunter levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Occultist Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you gain a Monster Hunter level, you can replace one spell on your list with another {@filter Wizard spell|spells|class=Wizard}.
details.spellcastingAbility: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your {@filter Wizard|spells|class=Wizard} spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your {@filter Wizard|spells|class=Wizard} spells.

features.name: Arcane Interference
features.description: You have {@status Advantage} on saving throws against spells cast by creature types in your Monster Grimoire. Additionally, when a creature you can see within 60 feet of you casts a spell or makes a spell attack, you can use Studied Response against that creature before the spell is cast.

features.name: Mage Hunter
features.description: You consider Humanoids that can cast spells as being a creature type in your Monster Grimoire. Additionally, when you damage a creature type in your Monster Grimoire that is {@status Concentrating|XPHB}, it has {@status Disadvantage} on the saving throw it makes to maintain its {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

features.name: Occult Knowledge
features.description: Your knowledge of magic has increased so that you learn to cast Rituals. You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and it's a spell you have prepared. In addition, you learn two spells of your choice. These spells can come from the {@filter Cleric|spells|class=Cleric}, {@filter Druid|spells|class=Druid}, or {@filter Wizard|spells|class=Wizard} spell list or any combination thereof. A spell you choose must have the Ritual tag. When you reach Monster Hunter level 14, you can replace one of the spells you know from this feature with another spell from any spell list. The new spell must have the Ritual tag.

features.name: Magical Aegis
features.description: You gain the ability to extend a protective charm. You radiate an unsealable aura in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} that originates from you. The aura is inactive while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. You and allies in your aura have {@status Advantage} on saving throws against spells cast by creature types in your Monster Grimoire. Additionally, you always have the {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} once without expending a spell slot. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Arcane Response
features.description: You have learned to anticipate your enemies well enough to rapidly cast spells in response to their attacks. When you use Studied Response, you can cast a spell. The spell must have a casting time of an action and must target only that creature.

name: Trapper Guild
description: Legends say that the founding members of the Trapper Guild were game hunters who had grown bored of stalking common elk and dreamed of taking down bigger game. Trappers take pride in their kills and the ingenuity exhibited when a trap works perfectly. The legacy of the Trapper Guild is a consistent testament to the benefits of lying in wait. The element of surprise is paramount to the trapper, whose kit can flourish before the hunter and monster even meet. Preparation is key, and the resourceful trapper has devices to assist in bringing down even the strongest foes.

features.name: Sneaky and Crafty
features.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill and with {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}.

features.name: Trapper Gadgets
features.description: You learn to create gadgets and mechanisms that help you during the hunt. As part of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can craft two Trapper Gadgets if you have materials and {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} on hand. Also, with 1 hour of work with such a kit and expending 20 GP worth of materials (such as equipment or monster salvage), you can create one Trapper Gadget from the {@filter Trapper Gadgets|optionalfeatures|feature type=MH:TG} list. Some Trapper Gadgets allow your target to make an ability check or saving throw to resist the gadget's effects. The saving throw DC is calculated as follows: {@b Trapper Gadgets save DC} = 8 + Intelligence modifier + {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

features.name: Ambusher's Advantage
features.description: You have become a ferocious ambusher. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your Intelligence modifier to the roll. Additionally, you can't be surprised by enemies that include creature types in your Monster Grimoire.

features.name: Agile Response
features.description: You can leap aside to avoid enemies rushing toward you. When a creature makes a melee attack roll against you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@status Disadvantage} on that roll and use your Studied Response as part of the same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. Whether the attack hits or misses, you can then move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

features.name: Monster-Hide Armor
features.description: You have learned to craft a set of Light or Medium armor by using dragon scales, werewolf hide, troll leather, or a similar monster component. The armor takes on the appearance of your choice, reflecting the component it is made from. The armor has the same properties as Light or Medium armor (your choice when the armor is crafted) and gains two modifications from the {@filter Armor Modifications|optionalfeatures|feature type=MH:AM} list. Whenever you gain a Monster Hunter level, you can replace one of these modifications with another modification. Modifications require the armor to be worn to function.

features.name: Rapid Tinkerer
features.description: You have become capable of crafting trapper tools at a much faster rate. You can spend 1 minute to make a Trapper Gadget without spending GP or components. You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Oath of the Ancients
description: The Oath of the Ancients is as old as the first elves and the rituals of Druids. Paladins who swear this oath cherish the light; they love the beautiful and life-giving things of the world more than any principles of honor, courage, and justice. They adorn their armor and clothing with images of growing things—leaves, antlers, or flowers—to reflect their commitment to preserving life and light in the world.

features.name: Oath of the Ancients Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Ancients Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Nature's Wrath
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to conjure spectral vines around nearby creatures. Each creature of your choice that you can see within 15 feet of you must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

features.name: Aura of Warding
features.description: Ancient magic lies so heavily upon you that it forms an eldritch ward, granting you and your allies in your Aura of Protection {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.

features.name: Undying Sentinel
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can choose to drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} instead, and you regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to three times your Paladin level. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Additionally, you can't be aged magically, and you cease visibly aging.

features.name: Elder Champion
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue your Aura of Protection with primal power, granting the benefits below for 1 minute or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Diminish Defiance.} Enemies in the aura have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your spells and Channel Divinity options. {@b Regeneration.} At the start of each of your turns, you regain 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. {@b Swift Spells.} Whenever you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can cast it using a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} instead.

name: Oath of Conquest
description: The Oath of Conquest calls to Paladins who seek glory in battle and the subjugation of their enemies. It isn't enough for these Paladins to establish order. They must crush the forces of chaos. These Paladins are often combatants in military orders, and their tenets emphasize discipline, courage, and ruthlessness.

features.name: Oath of Conquest Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Conquest Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Conquering Presence
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to exude a terrifying presence. As an action, you force each creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you for 1 minute. The {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

features.name: Guided Strike
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to strike with supernatural accuracy. When you make an attack roll, you can use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

features.name: Aura of Conquest
features.description: You emanate a menacing aura while you're not {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction but not through Total Cover. If a creature is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 while in the aura, and that creature takes Psychic damage equal to half your Paladin level if it starts its turn there. At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Scornful Rebuke
features.description: Those who dare to strike you are psychically punished for their audacity. Whenever a creature hits you with an attack, that creature takes Psychic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) if you're not {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

features.name: Invincible Conqueror
features.description: You gain the ability to harness extraordinary martial prowess. As an action, you can magically become an avatar of conquest, gaining the benefits below for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage. {@b Additional Attack.} When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can make one additional attack as part of that action. {@b Critical Strike.} Your melee weapon attacks score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

name: Oath of the Crown
description: The Oath of the Crown is sworn to the ideals of civilization, be it the spirit of a nation, fealty to a sovereign, or service to a deity of law and rulership. The Paladins who swear this oath dedicate themselves to serving society and, in particular, the just laws that hold society together.

features.name: Oath of the Crown Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Crown Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Champion Challenge
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you issue a challenge that compels other creatures to do battle with you. Each creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature can't willingly move more than 30 feet away from you. This effect ends on the creature if you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or die or if the creature is more than 30 feet away from you.

features.name: Turn the Tide
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can use your Channel Divinity to bolster injured creatures with your holy power. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you that can hear you regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) if it has no more than half of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

features.name: Divine Allegiance
features.description: When a creature within 5 feet of you takes damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to magically substitute your own health for that of the target creature, causing that creature not to take the damage. Instead, you take the damage. This damage to you can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

features.name: Unyielding Spirit
features.description: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid becoming {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.

features.name: Exalted Champion
features.description: Your presence on the field of battle is an inspiration to those dedicated to your cause. You can use your action to gain the benefits below for 1 hour. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage from nonmagical weapons. {@b Saving Throw Aura.} Your allies within 30 feet of you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws. {@b Healing Aura.} Whenever you or an ally starts their turn in the aura while at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the creature regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB}.

name: Oath of Devotion
description: The Oath of Devotion binds a Paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice and order. These Paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to the same standards. Many who swear this oath are devoted to gods of law and good and use their gods' tenets as the measure of their devotion.
shortDescription: Uphold the Ideals of Justice and Order

features.name: Oath of Devotion Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Devotion Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 10 minutes or until you use this feature again, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls you make with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1), and each time you hit with it, you cause it to deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. The weapon also emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} 20 feet beyond that. You can end this effect early (no action required). This effect also ends if you aren't carrying the weapon.

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition while in your Aura of Protection. If a {@condition Charmed|XPHB} ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there.

features.name: Smite of Protection
features.description: Your magical smite now radiates protective energy. Whenever you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you and your allies have {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} while in your Aura of Protection. The aura has this benefit until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue your Aura of Protection with holy power, granting the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Holy Ward.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any saving throw you are forced to make by a Fiend or an Undead. {@b Radiant Damage.} Whenever an enemy starts its turn in the aura, that creature takes Radiant damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. {@b Sunlight.} The aura is filled with {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} that is sunlight.

name: Oath of Glory
description: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism. They train diligently and encourage their companions so they're all ready when destiny calls. These Paladins share the following tenets: actions over words, challenges are but tests, and one must hone the body for it is a vessel for the soul.
shortDescription: Strive for the Heights of Heroism

features.name: Oath of Glory Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Glory Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Inspiring Smite
features.description: Immediately after you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and distribute {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} to creatures of your choice within 30 feet of yourself, which can include you. The total number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equals {@dice 2d8} plus your Paladin level, divided among the chosen creatures however you like.

features.name: Peerless Athlete
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to augment your athleticism. For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) and Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks, and the distance of your {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} and {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} increases by 10 feet (this extra distance costs movement as normal).

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, whenever an ally enters your Aura of Protection for the first time on a turn or starts their turn there, the ally's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: You can turn defense into a sudden strike. When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add your Charisma modifier to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss. If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, provided the attacker is within your weapon's range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: You can empower yourself with the legends—whether true or exaggerated—of your great deeds. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Charismatic.} You are blessed with an otherworldly presence and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Charisma checks. {@b Saving Throw Reroll.} If you fail a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll it. You must use the new roll. {@b Unerring Strike.} Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll with a weapon and miss, you can cause that attack to hit instead.

name: Oath of the Noble Genie
description: Paladins sworn to the Oath of the Noble Genies revere the forces of the Elemental Planes. Through taking this oath, Paladins draw power from the four different types of genies—dao, masters of earth, djinn, masters of air, efreet, masters of fire, and marids, masters of water. In Faerûn, many Paladins who swear this oath hail from Calimshan, a land teeming with genies.

features.name: Oath of the Noble Genies Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Noble Genies Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Elemental Smite
features.description: Immediately after you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and invoke one of the following effects.

details.daosCrush: {@b Dao's Crush.} Earth rises up around the target of your {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}. The target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape DC equal to your spell save DC). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.
details.djinnisEscape: {@b Djinni's Escape.} You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself and take on a semi-incorporeal form, which lasts until the end of your next turn. While in this form, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you have Immunity to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions.
details.efreetisFury: {@b Efreeti's Fury.} The target of your {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB} takes an extra {@damage 2d4} Fire damage, and fire jumps from the target to another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The second creature also takes {@damage 2d4} Fire damage.
details.maridsSurge: {@b Marid's Surge.} The target of your {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB} and each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature is pushed 15 feet straight away from you and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Genie's Splendor
features.description: When you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers. You can use a {@item Shield|XPHB} and still gain this benefit. You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

features.name: Aura of Elemental Shielding
features.description: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. You and your allies have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type while in your Aura of Protection. At the start of each of your turns, you can change the damage type affected by this feature to one of the other listed options (no action required).

features.name: Elemental Rebuke
features.description: When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to halve the attack's damage against yourself (round down) and force the attacker to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker takes damage equal to {@damage 2d10} plus your Charisma modifier of one of the following types (your choice): Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. On a successful save, the attacker takes half as much damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Noble Scion
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Flight.} You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet and can hover. {@b Minor Wish.} When you or an ally in your Aura of Protection fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make the {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} succeed instead.

name: Oath of Pestilence
description: Clad in grime-soaked armor and wielding rusting weapons, Oath of Pestilence Paladins spread corruption, disease, and filth. Bound by an oath that infests their bodies with all manner of plagues, these heralds of decay lumber forward with unholy toughness and grim resolve.

features.name: Oath of Pestilence Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Pestilence Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Debilitating Fever
features.description: You can inflict disease upon a creature. When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to give that creature the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, the target also has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. At the end of each of its turns, the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} target makes a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

features.name: Entropic Infection
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and select a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. For 1 minute, if you deal damage to the target, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage. In addition, the target loses {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and Immunity to Necrotic damage. The target can make a Constitution saving throw against the Paladin's spell save DC at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

features.name: Aura of Rampant Sickness
features.description: You emit an aura of contagion and virulence. When a creature within your Aura of Protection is about to make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}.

features.name: Disgusting Resilience
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can spend any number of Hit Dice, roll them, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled on those dice. In addition, if you are killed, your corpse explodes in a shower of pus and gore. Each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the Paladin's spell save DC, taking {@damage 8d6} Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

features.name: Plaguebringer
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b One with Plague.} You are Immune to Poison damage and the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. {@b Bolstered by Rot.} Your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum can't be reduced. {@b Entropic Radiance.} Whenever an enemy starts its turn within your Aura of Protection, it takes Necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

name: Oath of Redemption
description: The Oath of Redemption sets a Paladin on a difficult path, one that requires a holy warrior to use violence only as a last resort. Paladins who dedicate themselves to this oath believe that any person can be redeemed and that the path of benevolence and justice is one that anyone can walk.
shortDescription: Seek Peace and Redemption for All

features.name: Oath of Redemption Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Redemption Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Emissary of Peace
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to augment your presence with divine power. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you grant yourself a +5 bonus to Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks for the next 10 minutes.

features.name: Rebuke the Violent
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to rebuke those who use violence. Immediately after an attacker within 30 feet of you deals damage with an attack against a creature other than you, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the attacker takes Radiant damage equal to the damage it just dealt. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage.

features.name: Aura of the Guardian
features.description: You can shield your allies from harm at the cost of your own health. When a creature within your Aura of Protection takes damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to magically take that damage instead. This feature doesn't transfer any other effects that might accompany the damage, and this damage can't be reduced in any way.

features.name: Protective Spirit
features.description: A holy presence mends your wounds in battle. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} plus half your Paladin level if you end your turn in combat with fewer than half of your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} remaining and you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

features.name: Emissary of Redemption
features.description: You become an avatar of peace, which gives you the benefits below. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Damage Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage dealt by other creatures (their attacks, spells, and other effects). {@b Radiant Rebuke.} Whenever a creature damages you, it takes Radiant damage equal to half the amount it dealt to you. If you attack a creature, cast a spell on it, or deal damage to it by any means but this feature, neither benefit works against that creature until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Oath of Slaughter
description: The only truth of war is death. The only virtue is to kill. The only victory is to survive. Heroism, honor, and glory are illusions. This is the creed of Tormach, Arch Daemon of Wrath, but this philosophy rings true for all warriors who devote themselves, body and soul, to the thrill of combat and the delight of bloodshed. Such champions find themselves at the forefront of armies, using the most violent methods at their disposal to bring battles to a swift close... or drawing out the war to sate their own appetites.

features.name: Oath of Slaughter Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Slaughter Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Frenzied Slaughter
features.description: You can harness the oath of battle to drive you to even greater acts of violence. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to enter a battle frenzy. You gain the following benefits while this feature is active.

details.reflexiveAttack: {@b Reflexive Attack.} When you miss with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make another attack with the same weapon.
details.conditionResistance: {@b Condition Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB} conditions.
details.duration: {@b Duration.} Frenzied Slaughter lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. If your Frenzied Slaughter is still active on your next turn, you can extend it for another round by doing one of the following: make an attack roll against an enemy; force an enemy to make a saving throw; or you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} at the end of your turn. Each time Frenzied Slaughter is extended, it lasts until the end of your next turn. You can maintain it for up to 1 minute.

features.name: Bloodthirst Aura
features.description: Your lust for blood affects those around you. When a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} ally within your Aura of Protection makes an attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it gains a bonus to damage. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier. Additionally, when a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature within your Aura of Protection makes a saving throw against a Sanguimancy spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the save.

features.name: Follow Through
features.description: When a creature in your Aura of Protection becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and make a Melee weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

features.name: Blood Knight
features.description: Your bloodlust imbues you with preternatural strength and resilience, allowing you to keep sowing slaughter. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Crimson Armor.} When a creature within your Aura of Protection becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB} by an enemy, you gain 30 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. {@b Seeing Red.} When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. {@b Wanton Slaughter.} When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can choose any number of creatures within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. Each chosen creature takes Force damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1) plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

name: Oath of Vengeance
description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed grievously sinful acts. When evil armies slaughter helpless villagers, when a tyrant defies the will of the gods, when a thieves' guild grows too violent and powerful, when a dragon rampages through the countryside—at times like these, paladins arise and swear an Oath of Vengeance to set right that which has gone wrong.

features.name: Oath of Vengeance Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Vengeance Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Vow of Enmity
features.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the creature for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or falls {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}. If the creature drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} before the vow ends, you can transfer the vow to a different creature within 30 feet of yourself (no action required).

features.name: Relentless Avenger
features.description: Your supernatural focus helps you close off a foe's retreat. When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, you can reduce the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 until the end of the current turn. You can then move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} as part of the same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

features.name: Soul of Vengeance
features.description: The bond of your Vow of Enmity intensifies. When a creature under the effect of your Vow of Enmity makes an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack against that creature if it is within range.

features.name: Avenging Angel
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Flight.} You sprout spectral wings on your back, have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet, and can hover. {@b Frightful Aura.} Whenever an enemy starts its turn in your Aura of Protection, that creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. Attack rolls against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

name: Oath of the Watchers
description: The Oath of the Watchers binds Paladins to protect the mortal realm from the predations of extraplanar creatures, many of which can corrupt or destroy the unsuspecting. These Paladins are ever vigilant against the incursions of Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, and Fiends.

features.name: Oath of the Watchers Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Watchers Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Watcher's Will
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to invest your presence with the warding power of your faith. As an action, you choose a number of creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). For 1 minute, you and the chosen creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

features.name: Abjure the Extraplanar
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to castigate unworldly beings. As an action, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} and each Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, or Fiend within 30 feet of you that can hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0.

features.name: Aura of the Sentinel
features.description: You emit an aura of alertness while you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. When you and any creatures of your choice within your Aura of Protection roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, each of you gains a bonus to {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

features.name: Vigilant Rebuke
features.description: You've learned how to chastise anyone who dares wield beguilements against you and your wards. Whenever you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal {@damage 2d8} plus your Charisma modifier in Force damage to the creature that forced the saving throw.

features.name: Mortal Bulwark
features.description: You manifest a spark of divine power in defense of the mortal realm. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Truesight.} You gain {@variantrule Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet. {@b Advantage on Attacks.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, and Fiends. {@b Banishing Blow.} When you hit a creature with an attack and deal damage to it, you can force it to make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is magically banished to its native plane of existence if it's not currently there. On a successful save, the creature can't be banished by this feature for 24 hours.

name: Oath of Zeal
description: Zealots who take the Oath of Zeal are devoted to rooting out heresy and corruption wherever they find it. They serve as inquisitors, judges, and executioners, wielding divine authority to expose lies and punish the wicked. Their fervent devotion grants them supernatural insight and the power to compel truth from even the most stubborn deceivers.

features.name: Oath of Zeal Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Zeal Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Mark of the Heretic
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to mark a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself as a heretic. For 1 minute, your weapon attacks and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} against the chosen creature can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}. In addition, whenever the target starts its turn, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack against that creature if it's within reach.

features.name: Aura of Clarity
features.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition while in your Aura of Protection. If a {@condition Blinded|XPHB} ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there. Additionally, you can see {@condition Invisible|XPHB} creatures within your Aura of Protection.

features.name: Compel Confession
features.description: You can cast {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. In addition, a creature that succeeds on its saving throw takes {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage at the start of each of its turns while in your {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB} until it chooses to fail its saving throw instead.

features.name: Apocalyptic Revelation
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can reveal the true nature of your enemies for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). You gain the following benefits. {@b Blinding Glory.} Enemies that start their turn within 5 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw against the Paladin's spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. {@b See the Truth.} You have {@variantrule Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. {@b Smite the Heretic.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can choose a creature within 60 feet of yourself and reveal its weaknesses. You and your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature.

name: Beast Master
description: A Beast Master forms a mystical bond with a special animal, drawing on primal magic to strengthen both warrior and beast alike. These Rangers are adept at fighting alongside their bonded companion, and the primal beast grows in power alongside the Ranger.

features.name: Primal Companion
features.description: You magically summon a primal beast, which draws strength from your bond with nature. The beast is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. Choose its stat block—{@creature Beast of the Land|XPHB}, {@creature Beast of the Sea|XPHB}, or {@creature Beast of the Sky|XPHB}—which uses your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (PB) in several places. You also determine the kind of animal the beast is, choosing a kind appropriate for the stat block. Whatever kind you choose, the beast bears primal markings indicating its supernatural origin.

details.combat: In combat, the beast acts during your turn. It can move and use its {@action Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take a different action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. You can also sacrifice one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to command the beast to take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the beast acts on its own.
details.restoring: If the beast has died within the last hour, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch it and expend a spell slot. The beast returns to life after 1 minute with all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored.
details.replacing: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can summon a different primal beast, which appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. You choose its stat block and appearance. If you already have a beast from this feature, the old one vanishes when the new one appears.

features.name: Exceptional Training
features.description: When you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command your Primal Companion beast to take an action, you can also command it to take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, {@action Dodge|XPHB}, or {@action Help|XPHB} action using its {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. In addition, whenever it hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: When you command your Primal Companion beast to take the Beast's Strike action, the beast can use it twice. In addition, the first time each turn it hits a creature under the effect of your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell, the beast deals extra Force damage equal to the bonus damage of that spell.

features.name: Share Spells
features.description: When you cast a spell targeting yourself, you can also affect your Primal Companion beast with the spell if the beast is within 30 feet of you.

name: Drakewarden
description: Your connection to the natural world takes the form of a draconic spirit, which can manifest as a drake companion. As your powers grow, your drake companion grows as well, blossoming from a small creature into a majestic winged drake large enough to ride.

drakewardenOrigin.origin: You studied a dragon's scale or claw, or a trinket from a dragon's hoard, creating your bond through that token's lingering draconic magic.
A secret order of rangers who collect and protect dragon eggs recruited you, and your drake hatched from an egg in your care.
A dragon gave you a gift of power in thanks for a service you provided.
You drank a few drops of dragon blood, forever linking yourself to a dragon's power.
An ancient Draconic ritual was performed on you, binding dragon magic to your soul.
You have a latent draconic heritage that manifested as your drake companion.

features.name: Draconic Gift
features.description: The bond you share with your drake creates a connection to dragonkind, granting you understanding and empowering your presence. You gain the following benefits.

details.thaumaturgy: {@b Thaumaturgy.} You learn the {@spell Thaumaturgy|XPHB} cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.
details.tongueOfDragons: {@b Tongue of Dragons.} You learn to speak, read, and write {@language Draconic|XPHB} or one other language of your choice.

features.name: Drake Companion
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically summon the drake that is bound to you. It appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet of you. The drake is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. See its game statistics in the {@creature Drake Companion} stat block, which uses your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (PB) in several places. When you summon the drake, choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. The chosen type determines the drake's Damage Immunity and the damage of its Infused Strikes trait.

details.combat: In combat, the drake shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its {@action Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the drake can take any action of its choice, not just {@action Dodge|XPHB}.
details.duration: The drake remains until it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, until you use this feature to summon the drake again, or until you die. Anything the drake was wearing or carrying is left behind when the drake vanishes.
