details.resummon: Once you summon the drake, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to summon it again.

features.name: Bond of Fang and Scale
features.description: The bond you share with your drake intensifies, protecting you and stoking the drake's fury. When you summon your drake, it grows wings on its back and gains a flying speed equal to its walking speed. In addition, while your drake is summoned, you and the drake gain the following benefits.

details.drakeMountain: {@b Drake Mount.} The drake grows to Medium size. Reflecting your special bond, you can use the drake as a mount if your size is Medium or smaller. While you are riding your drake, it can't use the flying speed of this feature.
details.magicFang: {@b Magic Fang.} The drake's Bite attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the type chosen for the drake's Draconic Essence.
details.resistance: {@b Resistance.} You gain resistance to the damage type chosen for the drake's Draconic Essence.

features.name: Drake's Breath
features.description: As an action, you can exhale a 30-foot cone of damaging breath or cause your drake to exhale it. Choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (your choice doesn't have to match your drake's Draconic Essence). Each creature in the cone must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 8d6} damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This damage increases to {@damage 10d6} when you reach 15th level in this class. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to use it again.

features.name: Perfected Bond
features.description: Your bond to your drake reaches the pinnacle of its power. While your drake is summoned, you and the drake gain the following benefits.

details.empoweredBite: {@b Empowered Bite.} The drake's Bite attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the type chosen for its Draconic Essence (for a total of {@damage 2d6} extra damage).
details.largeDrake: {@b Large Drake.} The drake grows to Large size. When you ride your drake, it is no longer prohibited from using the flying speed of Bond of Fang and Scale.
details.reflexiveResistance: {@b Reflexive Resistance.} When either you or the drake takes damage while you're within 30 feet of each other, you can use your reaction to give yourself or the drake resistance to that instance of damage. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

name: Fey Wanderer
description: A Fey Wanderer Ranger's mystical wanderings in the Feywild have transformed them, granting the beguiling charm and dreadful power of that enchanted plane. Your weapons and magic carry the power of the Feywild, allowing you to guard the mortal realm against threats from beyond the veil.

feywildGifts.gift: Fresh flowers sprout from your hair each dawn.
You faintly smell of cinnamon, lavender, nutmeg, or another comforting herb or spice.
Your shadow dances while no one is looking directly at it.
Horns or antlers sprout from your head.
Your skin and hair change color to match the season at each dawn.

features.name: Dreadful Strikes
features.description: You can augment your weapon strikes with mind-scarring magic drawn from the gloomy hollows of the Feywild. When you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. The extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6} when you reach Ranger level 11.

features.name: Fey Wanderer Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Fey Wanderer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Otherworldly Glamour
features.description: Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +1). You also gain proficiency in one of these skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

features.name: Beguiling Twist
features.description: The magic of the Feywild guards your mind. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. In addition, whenever you or a creature you can see within 120 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to force a different creature you can see within 120 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

features.name: Fey Reinforcements
features.description: You can cast {@spell Summon Fey|XPHB} without a Material component. You can also cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.

features.name: Misty Wanderer
features.description: You can cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, whenever you cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, you can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of your destination space.

name: Gloom Stalker
description: Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places, wielding shadow magic to combat threats that lurk in the dark. These Rangers are adept at ambushes and striking from the shadows, drawing on the power of the Shadowfell to enhance their abilities.
shortDescription: Draw on Shadow Magic to Fight Your Foes

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description: You master the art of the ambush. You gain the following benefits.

details.ambushersLeap: {@b Ambusher's Leap.} At the start of your first turn of each combat, your {@variantrule Walking Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn.
details.dreadfulStrike: {@b Dreadful Strike.} When you attack a creature and hit it with a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage. You can use this benefit only once per turn, you can use it a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.initiativeBonus: {@b Initiative Bonus.} When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the roll.

features.name: Gloom Stalker Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Gloom Stalker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Umbral Sight
features.description: You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet. You are also adept at evading creatures that rely on {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}. While you are entirely in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition to any creature that relies on {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} to see you in that {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.

features.name: Iron Mind
features.description: You have honed your ability to resist mind-altering powers. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

features.name: Stalker's Flurry
features.description: The Psychic damage of your Dreadful Strike becomes {@damage 2d8}. In addition, when you use the Dreadful Strike effect of your Dread Ambusher feature, you can cause one of the following additional effects.

details.suddenStrike: {@b Sudden Strike.} You can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and that is within the weapon's range.
details.massFear: {@b Mass Fear.} The target and each creature within 10 feet of it must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Shadowy Dodge
features.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll. Whether the attack hits or misses, you can then teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

name: Green Reaper
description: A Green Reaper is an expert in the use of natural venoms and toxic substances, wielding the lethal power of nature's poisons to slay their foes. These Rangers harvest deadly compounds from plants and creatures, turning them into potent weapons.

features.name: Envenomed Attack
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can apply a natural venom to one weapon or piece of ammunition that you're holding. The next time you hit a creature with that weapon or ammunition, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Poison damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. This feature improves when you reach Ranger level 11: the Poison damage increases to {@damage 2d4}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Toxic Tradecraft
features.description: You gain proficiency with the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency with another type of Artisan's Tools of your choice. Whenever you make an ability check using the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} is doubled for the check. In addition, you've honed the ability to harvest and distill toxic components from plants and creatures. When you deal Poison damage to a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend a spell slot to infuse the attack with a concentrated toxin. The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Poison damage per level of the spell slot expended and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. A {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success. When the creature first fails this save, it also suffers a Toxin Effect determined by the level of the spell slot you expended, as shown in the Toxin Effects table.

level1.description: When you expend a level 1+ spell slot, choose one of the following effects.

effects.attenuate: {@b Attenuate.} While a creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from this toxin, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
effects.befuddled: {@b Befuddled.} While a creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from this toxin, it can't speak, read, or write and it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to maintain {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} on spells.
effects.uncoordinated: {@b Uncoordinated.} While a creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from this toxin, it can't take {@action Reactions|XPHB} or the {@action Disengage|XPHB} or {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.

level2.description: When you expend a level 2+ spell slot, choose one of the following effects.

effects.debilitate: {@b Debilitate.} For 1 minute, the creature can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
effects.potent: {@b Potent.} For 1 minute, the creature loses {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage.
effects.suffer: {@b Suffer.} For 1 minute, the first time on each turn the creature takes Poison damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Poison damage.

level3.description: When you expend a level 3+ spell slot, choose one of the following effects.

effects.lingering: {@b Lingering.} Instead of ending at the end of your next turn, the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 10 minutes. During this time it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws to end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on itself. At the end of each of the creature's turns, it can make a Constitution saving throw to end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
effects.supernatural: {@b Supernatural.} When you deal damage to a creature with a poisoned item, the creature loses Immunity to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and then has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of the creature's turns, it can make a Constitution saving throw to end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and regain its Immunity to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

level4.description: When you expend a level 4+ spell slot, choose one of the following effects.

effects.fleshEating: {@b Flesh Eating.} For 1 minute, the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. While the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from this toxin, it takes {@damage 2d6} Acid damage at the start of each of its turns.
effects.insensate: {@b Insensate.} While the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from this toxin, it also has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Deafened|XPHB} conditions.
effects.paraplegia: {@b Paraplegia.} While the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from this toxin, its Speed is reduced to 0 and it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.

level5.description: When you expend a level 5 spell slot, choose one of the following effects.

effects.enhanced: {@b Enhanced.} Apply one option from the Level 4 Spell Slot Effects list, and an additional option from either the Level 1 or Level 2 Spell Slot Effects list.
effects.stingingApplication: {@b Stinging Application.} For 1 minute, once per turn, when you hit a creature with a poisoned item, the attack deals an extra {@damage 5d6} damage. The damage type is your choice of Acid, Necrotic, or Poison.

features.name: Green Reaper Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Green Reaper Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Poison Control
features.description: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. In addition, you can cast the {@spell Protection from Poison|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Variegated Vexations
features.description: Whenever you would deal Poison damage with a weapon attack, you can change that damage to be either Acid or Necrotic instead.

features.name: Pain Tolerance
features.description: You have learned to quickly inure yourself against harm. Immediately before you take damage from a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage you take. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain at the end of your next turn, they vanish.

name: Horizon Walker
description: Horizon Walkers guard the world against threats that originate from other planes or that seek to ravage the mortal realm with otherworldly magic. They seek out planar portals and keep watch over them, venturing to the Inner Planes and the Outer Planes as needed to pursue their foes. These rangers are also friends to any forces in the multiverse—especially benevolent dragons, fey, and elementals—that work to preserve life and the order of the planes.
shortDescription: Guard the World Against Planar Threats

features.name: Horizon Walker Magic
features.description: Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Horizon Walker Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

features.name: Detect Portal
features.description: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to magically sense the presence of a planar portal. As an action, you detect the distance and direction to the closest planar portal within 1 mile of you. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. See the "Planar Travel" section in chapter 2 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for examples of planar portals.

features.name: Planar Warrior
features.description: At 3rd level, you learn to draw on the energy of the multiverse to augment your attacks. As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The next time you hit that creature on this turn with a weapon attack, all damage dealt by the attack becomes force damage, and the creature takes an extra 1d8 force damage from the attack. When you reach 11th level in this class, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Ethereal Step
features.description: At 7th level, you learn to step through the Ethereal Plane. As a bonus action, you can cast the {@spell Etherealness} spell with this feature, without expending a spell slot, but the spell ends at the end of the current turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Distant Strike
features.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to pass between the planes in the blink of an eye. When you take the {@action Attack} action, you can teleport up to 10 feet before each attack to an unoccupied space you can see. If you attack at least two different creatures with the action, you can make one additional attack with it against a third creature.

features.name: Spectral Defense
features.description: At 15th level, your ability to move between planes enables you to slip through the planar boundaries to lessen the harm done to you during battle. When you take damage from an attack, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to all of that attack's damage on this turn.

name: Hunter
description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect nature and people everywhere from forces that would destroy them.
shortDescription: Protect Nature and People from Destruction

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace the chosen option with the other one.

details.colossusSlayer: {@b Colossus Slayer.} Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage to the target if it's missing any of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.
details.hordeBreaker: {@b Horde Breaker.} Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

features.name: Hunter's Lore
features.description: You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace the chosen option with the other one.

details.escapeTheHorde: {@b Escape the Horde.} {@action Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.
details.multiattackDefense: {@b Multiattack Defense.} When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
features.description: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you can also deal that spell's extra damage to a different creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: When you take damage, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to give yourself {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage and any other damage of the same type until the end of the current turn.

name: Monster Slayer
description: You have dedicated yourself to hunting down creatures of the night and wielders of grim magic. A Monster Slayer seeks out vampires, dragons, evil fey, fiends, and other magical threats. Trained in supernatural techniques to overcome such monsters, slayers are experts at unearthing and defeating mighty, mystical foes.
shortDescription: Hunt Down Creatures of the Night

features.name: Monster Slayer Magic
features.description: Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Monster Slayer Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

features.name: Hunter's Sense
features.description: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to peer at a creature and magically discern how best to hurt it. As an action, choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You immediately learn whether the creature has any damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities and what they are. If the creature is hidden from divination magic, you sense that it has no damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Slayer's Prey
features.description: Starting at 3rd level, you can focus your ire on one foe, increasing the harm you inflict on it. As a bonus action, you designate one creature you can see within 60 feet of you as the target of this feature. The first time each turn that you hit that target with a weapon attack, it takes an extra 1d6 damage from the weapon. This benefit lasts until you finish a short or long rest. It ends early if you designate a different creature.

features.name: Supernatural Defense
features.description: At 7th level, you gain extra resilience against your prey's assaults on your mind and body. Whenever the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw and whenever you make an ability check to escape that target's grapple, add 1d6 to your roll.

features.name: Magic-User's Nemesis
features.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to thwart someone else's magic. When you see a creature casting a spell or teleporting within 60 feet of you, you can use your reaction to try to magically foil it. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC, or its spell or teleport fails and is wasted. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Slayer's Counter
features.description: At 15th level, you gain the ability to counterattack when your prey tries to sabotage you. If the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against the quarry. You make this attack immediately before making the saving throw. If your attack hits, your save automatically succeeds, in addition to the attack's normal effects.

name: Primordial Archer
description: In the areas of Etharis where nature still holds sway, many cling to the ancient customs of woodland hunters and witches. Marking themselves with wild plants and bone needles, theirs is a tradition of cunning, cruelty, and reprisal against all trespassers. Unlike the Druids, these wood witches twist natural magic into hexes, imbuing their weapons with nature's fury.
shortDescription: Channel the Wrath of the Wilds

features.name: Elemental Arrows
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue a Longbow or Shortbow with elemental energy for 1 minute. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. For the duration, the imbued weapon deals damage of the selected type instead of its normal type and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the chosen type when it hits. At the start of each of your turns, you can change this choice. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Herbal Lore
features.description: You gain an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} and are proficient with it. You can use the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to stabilize an Unconscious creature within 5 feet of you that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as if using a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} without needing to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. If you take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, an Unconscious creature within 5 feet of you that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} gains 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Primordial Archer Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Primordial Archer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Weave the Elements
features.description: With 1 hour of work or when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} to mark yourself with elemental patterns. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

features.name: Witching Arrows
features.description: You gain the ability to imbue curses into your arrows. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a ranged attack using a Longbow or Shortbow, you can expend a level 1+ spell slot to choose one of the following effects (no action required). The listed damage increases by {@damage 2d6} for each spell slot above 1.

details.arcingShot: {@b Arcing Shot.} Electricity crackles around the arrow. Make a ranged attack roll with the same weapon against a second creature within 30 feet of the first that is also within your range. Each creature hit takes {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage.
details.entanglingShot: {@b Entangling Shot.} The wooden shaft of the arrow sprouts tiny green leaves. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes the damage only. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
details.hexingShot: {@b Hexing Shot.} The magic surrounding your arrow clouds the mind of your target. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage and gains the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute (your choice). The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.
details.viperShot: {@b Viper Shot.} The arrow transforms into a hissing serpent. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Poison damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

features.name: Primordial Magic
features.description: Taking damage can't break your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} on any Ranger spells you cast. In addition, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to change the damage type you chose for the Weave the Elements feature to a different damage type in the list. When you do, you can choose a creature that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} damage of either damage type (your choice). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Swarmkeeper
description: Feeling a deep connection to the environment around them, some rangers reach out through their magical connection to the world and bond with a swarm of nature spirits. The swarm becomes a potent force in battle, as well as helpful company for the ranger. Some Swarmkeepers are outcasts or hermits, keeping to themselves and their attendant swarms rather than dealing with the discomfort of others. Other Swarmkeepers enjoy building vibrant communities that work for the mutual benefit of all those they consider part of their swarm.
shortDescription: Bond with a Swarm of Nature Spirits

features.name: Gathered Swarm
features.description: A swarm of intangible nature spirits has bonded itself to you and can assist you in battle. While you're alive, the swarm remains in your space, crawling on you or flying and skittering around you within your space. You determine its appearance, or you generate its appearance by rolling on the Swarm Appearance table. Once on each of your turns, you can cause the swarm to assist you in one of the following ways, immediately after you hit a creature with an attack: the attack's target takes 1d6 piercing damage from the swarm; the attack's target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be moved by the swarm up to 15 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice; or you are moved by the swarm 5 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice.

features.name: Swarmkeeper Magic
features.description: You learn the {@spell Mage Hand} cantrip if you don't already know it. When you cast it, the hand takes the form of your swarming nature spirits. You also learn an additional spell of 1st level or higher when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Swarmkeeper Spells table. Each spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

features.name: Writhing Tide
features.description: You can condense part of your swarm into a focused mass that lifts you up. As a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 10 feet and can hover. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Mighty Swarm
features.description: Your Gathered Swarm grows mightier in the following ways: the damage of Gathered Swarm increases to 1d8; if a creature fails its saving throw against being moved by Gathered Swarm, you can also cause the swarm to knock the creature prone; when you are moved by Gathered Swarm, it gives you half cover until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Swarming Dispersal
features.description: You can discorporate into your swarm, avoiding danger. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that damage. You vanish into your swarm and then teleport to an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of you, where you reappear with the swarm. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

name: Vermin Lord
description: Vermin Lords cultivate loyal hordes of disease-bearing rodents to help them battle greater evils. For many of these Rangers, that greater evil is some kind of societal ill, but a rare few see their fellow Humanoids as an infestation that threatens the natural world. Regardless of their goal, they make their homes in sewers, slums, and other forgotten places where they are free to plot against their enemies and tend to their verminkin.
shortDescription: Grow Powerful from Strength in Numbers

features.name: Verminkin
features.description: You can comprehend and verbally communicate with Tiny Beasts. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a spell slot to summon vermin swarms. You summon a number of swarms equal to the slot's level for 1 hour. Each swarm is summoned to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself. A swarm uses the {@creature Swarm of Vermin} stat block. In combat, each swarm acts during your turn. It can move and use its {@action Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. You can command each swarm with a single {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Swarming Strikes
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can command all of your swarms to make an {@action Attack|XPHB} action instead of just one. Alternatively, a single swarm can attack twice instead of once when it takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Vermin Lord Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Vermin Lord Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Filth and Fortitude
features.description: The time you've spent with plague-bearing rodents has rendered you Immune to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. Additionally, you gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws.

features.name: Infectious Spread
features.description: When you use Swarming Strikes, each swarm that takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action makes one additional attack. Each creature damaged by a swarm's attack during this action has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Strength of the Swarm
features.description: You can call on your rodent minions for defense. When you take damage from a creature you can see within 10 feet of yourself, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to direct the damage toward a swarm you control you can see within 5 feet of yourself.

name: Winter Walker
description: Winter Walkers hone their craft in the bleak and frozen wilds of places like Icewind Dale. These ruthless, rimed Rangers hunt monsters that haunt arctic wastelands, eventually becoming frigid terrors themselves. Winter Walkers are well versed in the phenomena of Icewind Dale, including the latent magic of fallen Netherese cities, endemic monsters like yetis and crag cats, and the rising threat of Underdark invaders. Due to their cold pragmatism, terrifying magic, and mastery of the region, Winter Walkers are regarded with equal parts respect and fear.
shortDescription: Withstand the Horrors of Frigid Wastelands

features.name: Frigid Explorer
features.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.bitingCold: {@b Biting Cold.} Damage from your weapon attacks, Ranger spells, and Ranger features ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
details.frostResistance: {@b Frost Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
details.polarStrikes: {@b Polar Strikes.} When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Cold damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. When you reach Ranger level 11, this extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6}.

features.name: Hunter's Rime
features.description: Ice rimes you and your prey, protecting you and slowing them. When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d10} plus your Ranger level. Additionally, while a creature is marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, it can't take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action.

features.name: Winter Walker Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Winter Walker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Fortifying Soul
features.description: Your experience surviving harrowing environments allows you to bolster your allies in addition to yourself. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a number of creatures you can see equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Each chosen creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d10} plus your Ranger level and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Chilling Retribution
features.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. While the target is {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, its Speed is reduced to 0 feet. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Frozen Haunt
features.description: When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you can adopt a ghostly, snowy form. This form lasts until the spell ends, and while you are in this form, you gain the following benefits. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required).

details.frozenSoul: {@b Frozen Soul.} You have Immunity to Cold damage. When you first adopt this form and at the start of each of your subsequent turns, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you takes {@damage 2d4} Cold damage.
details.partiallyIncorporeal: {@b Partially Incorporeal.} You have Immunity to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, but you take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object. If the form ends while you are inside a creature or an object, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space.

name: Arcane Trickster
description: Some Rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with Arcane spells, learning tricks of enchantment and illusion. These Rogues include pickpockets and burglars, but also pranksters and a significant number of adventurers.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells. The information below details how you use those rules with Wizard spells, which appear in the Wizard spell list.

details.cantrips: You know three cantrips: {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} and two other cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard}. Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one of your cantrips, except {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB}, with another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
details.preparedSpells: The Arcane Trickster Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of level 1 and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a Long Rest. You prepare the list of Wizard spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard}. The number of spells you can prepare is shown in the Prepared Spells column of the table. Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Wizard spell for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard spells.

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: When you cast {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB}, you can make the spectral hand invisible. You can perform the following additional tasks with it: you can stow one object the hand is holding in a container worn or carried by another creature; you can retrieve an object in a container worn or carried by another creature; you can use {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB} to pick locks and disarm traps at range.

name: Assassin
description: An Assassin's training focuses on the grim art of death, using stealth, poison, and disguise to eliminate foes with deadly efficiency. While some Assassins are hired killers, espionage agents, or bounty hunters, many serve as the hidden blade of a righteous cause.
shortDescription: Practice the Grim Art of Death

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: You are at your deadliest when you get the drop on your enemies. You have the following benefits.

details.initiativeAdvantage: {@b Initiative.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.
details.surprisingStrikes: {@b Surprising Strikes.} During the first turn of each combat, you deal extra damage to any creature you hit with an attack roll against a creature that hasn't taken a turn yet. The extra damage equals your Sneak Attack damage.

features.name: Assassin's Tools
features.description: You gain a proficiency you don't already have with the {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB} and the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}.

name: Highway Rider
description: Stalking the backroads, the Highway Rider strikes fear into the heart of every traveler and penny-pinching merchant. They run down their prize astride a swift and loyal steed—and then make a quick getaway.
shortDescription: A Steed Makes the Best Partner in Crime

features.name: Hair Trigger
features.description: You gain proficiency with Blackpowder Pistols. In addition, when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll, you can immediately take a Reaction for one of the following options: make one attack with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}; move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}; a controlled mount moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}; or take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} or {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.

features.name: Trusty Mount
features.description: You always have the {@spell Find Steed|XPHB} spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast it without a spell slot or components, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Ride Them Down
features.description: You don't need {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll to use your Sneak Attack if you or a controlled mount you ride moves at least 20 feet, and you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

name: Inquisitive
description: As an Inquisitive, you excel at rooting out secrets and unraveling mysteries. You rely on your sharp eye for detail and your finely honed ability to read the words and deeds of other creatures to determine their true intent. You excel at defeating creatures that hide among and prey upon ordinary folk, and your mastery of lore and your keen deductions make you well equipped to expose and end hidden evils.
shortDescription: Root Out Secrets and Unravel Mysteries

features.name: Ear for Deceit
features.description: Whenever you make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check to determine whether a creature is lying to you, treat a roll of 7 or lower on the {@dice d20} as an 8.

features.name: Eye for Detail
features.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check to spot a hidden creature or object or to make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to uncover or decipher clues.

features.name: Insightful Fighting
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check against a creature you can see that isn't {@condition Incapacitated}, contested by the target's Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. If you succeed, you can use your Sneak Attack against that target even if you don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, but not if you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on it. This benefit lasts for 1 minute or until you successfully use this feature against a different target.

name: Mastermind
description: Your focus is on people and on the influence and secrets they have. Many spies, courtiers, and schemers follow this archetype, leading lives of intrigue. Words are your weapons as often as knives or poison, and secrets and favors are some of your favorite treasures.

features.name: Master of Intrigue
features.description: You gain proficiency with the {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, the {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}, and one {@filter Gaming Set|items|type=gaming set} of your choice. You also learn two languages of your choice. Additionally, you can unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute, enabling you to pass yourself off as a native speaker of a particular land, provided that you know the language.

features.name: Master of Tactics
features.description: You can use the {@action Help|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Additionally, when you use the Help action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target of that attack can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet of you, if the target can see or hear you.

name: Misfortune Bringer
description: You've matched your penchant for illicit activities with the ability to mark your enemies for mishap and misfortune. Whether you were imbued with magic by spending time among the Fey or you learned the art of cursing from a long line of hedge Wizards, you are a Misfortune Bringer. Although not universal, many Misfortune Bringers possess heterochromia iridum—eyes of two different colors—and they use only one when glaring at targets they intend to curse.
shortDescription: Curse Those You're About to Strike

features.name: Evil Eye
features.description: You can place a minor curse with a glance. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself to be cursed by your Evil Eye. While a creature is cursed by your Evil Eye, you can deal Sneak Attack damage to the creature if you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. The creature remains cursed by your Evil Eye for 1 minute or until you curse a different creature with your Evil Eye, whichever comes first.

features.name: Misfortunist
features.description: You learn Misfortunes that you can inflict on those cursed by your Evil Eye.

details.misfortunes: You learn two Misfortunes of your choice, which are detailed under 'Misfortunes' below. You learn an additional Misfortune of your choice when you reach Rogue levels 9, 13, and 17. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one Misfortune you know with a different one.
details.jinxPoints: You have 4 Jinx Points. You gain 2 additional Jinx Points at Rogue level 13. To use a Misfortune option, you must spend the number of Jinx Points that it costs. You regain all expended Jinx Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.savingThrows: If a Misfortune requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your Charisma or Intelligence modifier (your choice) + your Proficiency Bonus.

name: Phantom
description: Many Rogues walk a fine line between life and death, risking their own lives and taking the lives of others. While adventuring on that line, some Rogues discover a mystical connection to death itself. These Rogues take knowledge from the dead and become combatants imbued with the power of death.

features.name: Whispers of the Dead
features.description: Echoes of those who have died cling to you. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it, as a ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this feature to choose a different proficiency that you lack.

features.name: Wails from the Grave
features.description: As you nudge someone closer to the grave, you can channel the power of death to harm someone else as well. Immediately after you deal your Sneak Attack damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll half the number of Sneak Attack dice for your level (round up), and the second creature takes necrotic damage equal to the roll's total, as wails of the dead sound for a moment. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Sanguine Thief
description: In dark alleyways across Etharis, deals are made in coin and blood, but a few enterprising Rogues have posed the question: 'Why not both?' Sanguine Thieves are assassins who harness the power of Sanguromancy to fuel their abilities, finding new and innovative ways to utilize the blood they see all too frequently in their line of work. Sanguine Thieves learn to draw upon the vitae of their victims, extending their ability to use blood magic beyond the limits of their own vitality. When cornered, Sanguine Thieves can unleash this power in a hail of crimson darts or fade before a foe's blade, leaving nothing behind but a red mist.
shortDescription: Eliminate Your Prey with Blood Magic

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast Wizard spells, as well as spells from the Sanguromancy school. All Sanguromancy spells and cantrips are treated as Wizard spells for the purposes of this subclass.

details.cantrips: You know three cantrips from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard} and from the list of Sanguromancy spells. Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another Wizard cantrip of your choice. When you reach Rogue level 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
details.spellSlots: The Sanguine Thief Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 Wizard spells. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Rogue levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Sanguine Thief Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Wizard spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Sanguine Thief Spellcasting table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Wizard spell for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard spells.

features.name: Stolen Power
features.description: You draw magic from blood. It is represented by your Sanguromancy Dice, which fuel powers you have from this subclass. You have a pool of {@dice d8}s that you can use on Sanguine Thief features. The number of dice in the pool equals the number of damage dice as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table. You can't have more Sanguromancy Dice than the number shown in the Sneak Attack column for your level, unless you have Sanguromancy Dice from a different source.

details.bloodMagic: You can spend Sanguromancy Dice instead of {@variantrule Hit Dice|XPHB} when you cast Sanguromancy spells.
details.recovery: Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Steal Blood
features.description: When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can restore 1 Sanguromancy Die. If you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, instead of restoring 1 Sanguromancy Die when you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can immediately roll the die and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Scion of the Three
description: A Scion of the Three draws power from a group of malevolent gods known as the Dead Three: Bane, deity of tyranny; Bhaal, deity of violence and murder; and Myrkul, deity of death. While some Rogues of this subclass pledge themselves ardently to those three macabre gods, others find themselves thrust on this path by a curse. Either way, a scion's power manifests as various occult gifts, as well as an uncanny talent for striking and terrifying foes.
shortDescription: Channel the Power of the Dead Three

features.name: Dread Allegiance
features.description: You forge a dark pact with one of the Dead Three, gaining occult gifts. Choose one of the following allegiances. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for the cantrip you gain from this feature.

details.bane: {@b Bane.} You gain Resistance to Psychic damage and learn the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip.
details.bhaal: {@b Bhaal.} You gain Resistance to Poison damage and learn the {@spell Blade Ward|XPHB} cantrip.
details.myrkul: {@b Myrkul.} You gain Resistance to Necrotic damage and learn the {@spell Chill Touch|XPHB} cantrip.

features.name: Bloodthirst
features.description: The Dead Three grant you the power to cull the weak, giving you the following benefits.

details.bonusDamage: {@b Bonus Damage.} When your Sneak Attack hits a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature, the target takes extra damage of the weapon's type equal to half your Rogue level (round up).
details.teleportStrike: {@b Teleport Strike.} When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you takes damage and is {@status Bloodied|XPHB} after taking that damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of that creature and make one melee attack against it. You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Strike Fear
features.description: Your Cunning Strike gains the following additional option.

details.terrify: {@b Terrify (Cost: 1d6).} The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the target. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

features.name: Aura of Malevolence
features.description: Your Bloodthirst's Teleport Strike now radiates deadly energy. When you teleport using Teleport Strike, each creature of your choice within 10 feet of the space you left or the space you arrive in takes damage equal to your Intelligence modifier. The damage type is determined by your current Dread Allegiance: Psychic for Bane, Poison for Bhaal, or Necrotic for Myrkul. This damage ignores Resistance.

features.name: Dread Incarnate
features.description: You become a living avatar of the Dead Three, gaining the following benefits.

details.cutthroat: {@b Cutthroat.} When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain one expended use of Bloodthirst's Teleport Strike.
details.murderousIntent: {@b Murderous Intent.} When you roll a Sneak Attack damage die and get a 1 or 2, you can treat the die as a 3.

name: Scout
description: You are skilled in stealth and surviving far from the streets of a city, allowing you to scout ahead of your companions during expeditions. Rogues who embrace this archetype are at home in the wilderness and among barbarians and rangers, and many Scouts serve as the eyes and ears of war bands. Ambusher, spy, bounty hunter—these are just a few of the roles that Scouts assume as they range the world.

features.name: Skirmisher
features.description: You are difficult to pin down during a fight. You can move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when an enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

features.name: Survivalist
features.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Nature} and {@skill Survival} skills if you don't already have it. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.

features.name: Superior Mobility
features.description: Your walking {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. If you have a Climb or Swim Speed, this increase applies to that {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} as well.

features.name: Ambush Master
features.description: You excel at leading ambushes and acting first in a fight. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls. In addition, the first creature you hit during the first round of a combat becomes easier for you and others to strike; attack rolls against that target have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Sudden Strike
features.description: You can strike with deadly speed. If you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can make one additional attack as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. This attack can benefit from your Sneak Attack even if you have already used it this turn, but you can't use your Sneak Attack against the same target more than once in a turn.

name: Soulknife
description: A Soulknife strikes with the mind, cutting through barriers both physical and psychic. These Rogues discover psionic power within themselves and channel it to do their roguish work. They find easy employment as members of thieves' guilds, though they are often mistrusted, and most combatants find a fight against a Soulknife to be an unsettling experience.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. It is represented by your Psionic Energy Dice, which fuel various powers you have from this subclass. The Soulknife Energy Dice table shows the die size and number of these dice you have when you reach certain Rogue levels.

details.psiBolsteredKnack: {@b Psi-Bolstered Knack.} If you fail an ability check using a skill or tool with which you have proficiency, you can roll one Psionic Energy Die and add the number rolled to the check, potentially turning failure into success. You expend the die only if the roll then succeeds.
details.psychicWhispers: {@b Psychic Whispers.} You can establish telepathic communication between yourself and others. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose one or more creatures you can see, up to a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus, and then roll one Psionic Energy Die. For a number of hours equal to the number rolled, the chosen creatures can speak telepathically with you, and you can speak telepathically with them. To send or receive a message (no action required), you and the other creature must be within 1 mile of each other. A creature can end the telepathic connection at any time (no action required). The first time you use this power after each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you don't expend the Psionic Energy Die. All other times you use the power, you expend the die.

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: You can manifest shimmering blades of psychic energy. Whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action or make an {@variantrule Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, you can manifest a psychic blade in your free hand and make the attack with that blade. The magic blade has the following traits.

details.stats: The blade deals {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage on a hit and has the {@variantrule Finesse|XPHB} and {@variantrule Thrown|XPHB} properties, with a normal range of 60 feet and no long range. The blade vanishes the instant after it hits or misses its target. It leaves no mark on its target.
details.bonusAttack: {@b Bonus Attack.} After you attack with the blade on your turn, you can make a melee or ranged attack with a second psychic blade as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on the same turn, provided your other hand is free to create it. The damage die of this bonus attack is {@damage 1d4}, instead of {@damage 1d6}.

features.name: Soul Blades
features.description: Your Psychic Blades are now an expression of your psi-suffused soul, giving you the following powers that use your Psionic Energy Dice.

details.homingStrikes: {@b Homing Strikes.} If you make an attack roll with your Psychic Blade and miss the target, you can roll one Psionic Energy Die and add the number rolled to the attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, you expend the Psionic Energy Die.
details.psychicTeleportation: {@b Psychic Teleportation.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you manifest a Psychic Blade, expend one Psionic Energy Die and roll it, and throw the blade at an unoccupied space you can see up to a number of feet away equal to 10 times the number rolled. You then teleport to that space, and the blade vanishes.

features.name: Psychic Veil
features.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can magically become {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, along with anything you are wearing or carrying, for 1 hour. This {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition ends early immediately after you deal damage to a creature or you force a creature to make a saving throw. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Rend Mind
features.description: When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can force that target to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your proficiency bonus plus your Dexterity modifier). If the save fails, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The {@condition Stunned|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend three Psionic Energy Dice (no action required) to restore your use of it.

name: Swashbuckler
description: You focus your training on the art of the blade, relying on speed, elegance, and charm in equal parts. While some warriors are brutes clad in heavy armor, your method of fighting looks almost like a performance. Duelists and pirates typically belong to this archetype. A Swashbuckler excels in single combat, and can fight with two weapons while safely darting away from an opponent.
shortDescription: Fight with Speed, Elegance, and Charm

features.name: Fancy Footwork
features.description: During your turn, if you make a melee attack against a creature, that creature can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against you for the rest of your turn.

features.name: Rakish Audacity
features.description: Your confidence propels you into battle. You can give yourself a bonus to your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls equal to your Charisma modifier. You also gain an additional way to use your Sneak Attack; you don't need {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll to use your Sneak Attack against a creature if you are within 5 feet of it, no other creature is within 5 feet of you, and you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. All the other rules for Sneak Attack still apply to you.

features.name: Panache
features.description: Your charm becomes extraordinarily beguiling. As an action, you can make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check contested by a creature's Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. The creature must be able to hear you, and the two of you must share a language.

details.hostileCreature: {@b Hostile Creature.} If you succeed against a Hostile creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against targets other than you and can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against targets other than you. This effect lasts for 1 minute, until one of your companions attacks the target or affects it with a spell, or until you and the target are more than 60 feet apart.
details.nonHostileCreature: {@b Other Creatures.} If you succeed against a creature that isn't Hostile to you, it is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you for 1 minute. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the creature regards you as a friendly acquaintance. This effect ends immediately if you or your companions do anything harmful to it.

features.name: Elegant Maneuver
features.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) or Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check you make during the same turn.

features.name: Master Duelist
features.description: Your mastery of the blade lets you turn failure into success in combat. If you miss with an attack roll, you can roll it again with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. Once you do so, you can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

name: Thief
description: A mix of burglar, pickpocket, and explorer, a Thief is a Rogue who tends to use stealth and dexterity over brute force. You are adept at quickly taking items and getting into places others can't reach, drawing on skills of infiltration and quick reflexes.
shortDescription: Use Nimbleness and Stealth to Steal Anything

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can do one of the following.

details.sleightOfHand: {@b Sleight of Hand.} Make a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check to pick a lock or disarm a trap with {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB} or to pick a pocket.
details.useAnObject: {@b Use an Object.} Take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, or take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to use a magic item that requires that action.

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.description: You have gained the following movement abilities.

details.climber: {@b Climber.} You gain a Climb Speed equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
details.jumpDistance: {@b Jump's Distance.} When you take the {@action Jump|XPHB} action, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on every Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check you make, provided you aren't wearing Medium or Heavy armor.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: In your treasure hunting, you have learned how to maximize use of magic items, giving you the following benefits.

details.attunement: {@b Attunement.} You can attune to up to four magic items at once.
details.charges: {@b Charges.} Whenever you use a magic item property that expends charges, roll a {@dice d6}. On a roll of 6, you use the property without expending the charges.
details.scrolls: {@b Scrolls.} You can use any {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability for the spell. If the spell is a cantrip or a level 1 spell, you can cast it reliably. If the spell is level 2 or higher, you must succeed on an Intelligence check with a DC of 10 plus the spell's level. On a failed check, the spell disappears from the scroll with no other effect.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: You have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and your second turn at your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} minus 10.

name: Aberrant Mind
description: An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you. Will this power shine from you as a hopeful beacon to others? Or will you be a terror to those who feel the stab of your mind? Perhaps a psychic wind from the Astral Plane carried psionic energy to you, or you were exposed to the Far Realm's warping influence. Alternatively, you were implanted with a mind flayer tadpole, but your transformation into a mind flayer never occurred; now the tadpole's psionic power is yours. However you acquired this power, your mind is aflame with it.

features.name: Psionic Spells
features.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Psionic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Telepathic Speech
features.description: You can form a telepathic connection between your mind and the mind of another. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically with each other while the two of you are within a number of miles of each other equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile). To understand each other, you each must mentally use a language the other knows. The telepathic connection lasts for a number of minutes equal to your Sorcerer level. It ends early if you use this ability to form a connection with a different creature.

features.name: Psionic Sorcery
features.description: When you cast any level 1+ spell from your Psionic Spells feature, you can cast it by expending a spell slot as normal or by spending a number of Sorcery Points equal to the spell's level. If you cast the spell using Sorcery Points, it requires no Verbal or Somatic components, and it requires no Material components unless they are consumed by the spell or have a cost specified in it.

features.name: Psychic Defenses
features.description: You have Resistance to Psychic damage, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Revelation in Flesh
features.description:
You can unleash the aberrant truth hidden within yourself. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point or more to magically alter your body for 10 minutes. For each Sorcery Point you spend, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice, the effects of which last until the alteration ends.

{@b Aquatic Adaptation.} You gain a Swim Speed equal to twice your Speed, and you can breathe underwater. Gills grow from your neck or flare behind your ears, and your fingers become webbed or you grow wriggling cilia.

{@b Glistening Flight.} You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed, and you can hover. As you fly, your skin glistens with mucus or otherworldly light.

{@b See the Invisible.} You can see any {@condition Invisible|XPHB} creature within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind Total Cover. Your eyes also turn black or become writhing sensory tendrils.

{@b Wormlike Movement.} Your body, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, becomes slimy and pliable. You can move through any space as narrow as 1 inch, and you can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Warping Implosion
features.description:
You can unleash a space-warping anomaly. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 120 feet of yourself. Immediately after you disappear, each creature within 30 feet of the space you left must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes {@dice 3d10} Force damage and is pulled straight toward the space you left, ending in an unoccupied space as close to your former space as possible. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

name: Apocalypse
description: Apocalypse Sorcerers have seen the shape of things to come. They may have visions of the Great Beast ushering in the end of Etharis or dreams of Gormadraug waking and devouring the land. These prophecies besiege Apocalypse Sorcerers, plaguing and empowering in equal measure. Some find relief in putting these revelations into writing, producing texts that read as incoherent but slowly seep into the mind as sensible, even inevitable. When the apocalypse-bringer is freed to travel across Etharis unburdened, undoing the works of gods and men, magic will swell and pool, ripe for the taking. This sign of the end is also their gift.
shortDescription: Hasten or Stall the End of the World

features.name: Apocalyptic Spells
features.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Apocalyptic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Unhinged Asseverations
features.description: You are obsessed with documenting your visions. You gain proficiency with {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB} and you can create {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB|Spell Scrolls} in half the time and at half the cost in GP. In addition, when you create a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, you can ensorcell it. When you ensorcell a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, you must expend a spell slot equal to or greater than the spell's level and you can spend Sorcery Points to apply one of your Metamagic options. Any creature that knows at least one language can use your ensorcelled {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, which casts the spell with the benefit of the Metamagic option you chose. The {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} remains ensorcelled until it is used or you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Bear Witness
features.description:
You have prepared your entire life for the end of the world. While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you gain the following benefits:

**Apocalyptic Inurement.** You have Resistance to Force damage.

**Recite Scripture.** Once per active Innate Sorcery, as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} you can use a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} that has a spell with a casting time of {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

**Unflappable.** You are Immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Arcane Apocrypha
features.description: Your obsessive reflections on the end of existence inspire your writing. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} at no cost. It must be a spell of level 5 or lower that you can cast. This {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} disintegrates when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Forbidden Magic
features.description:
The end of Etharis is a time when magic is unbound and magical powers long hidden are unearthed. When you cast a Sorcerer spell using a spell slot, you can choose one option below.

{@b Excessive.} If the spell requires a Material component with a cost, you can cast the spell without the Material component. You take Force damage equal to four times the level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity.

{@b Inexorable.} Taking damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell. When the spell ends, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

{@b Pyrrhic.} If the spell requires an attack roll, the spell automatically hits and the attack roll is a Critical Hit. Your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum is reduced by an amount equal to four times the level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, your Hit Point maximum returns to normal.

features.name: The End is Nigh
features.description:
You loudly proclaim what will come to pass when the world ends. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you describe the end of days. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes {@dice 6d6} Psychic damage and {@dice 6d6} Force damage and has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only. A {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition on a successful save. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the creature can be revived only by a {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} or a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you spend 6 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

name: Clockwork Soul
description: The cosmic force of order has suffused you with magic. That power arises from Mechanus or a realm like it—a plane of existence shaped entirely by clockwork efficiency. You or someone from your lineage might have become entangled in the machinations of Modrons. Or perhaps you were exposed to the realm's raw clockwork while at a young age. Whatever its origin, the power simmers in you, and your spells emerge in spectral gear wheels and tiny cogs in the air.
